# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUBC — BUK Bootstrap Constants
# Source-time literal constants for BUK kit layout.
# No kindle dependency — available immediately upon sourcing.

# Guard against multiple inclusion
test -z "${ZBUBC_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZBUBC_SOURCED=1

# Source-time literal constants
BUBC_rbmn_nodes_subdir="rbmn_nodes"
BUBC_rbmu_users_subdir="rbmu_users"

# Platform identifiers (bubep_* — bub/bube/bubep_* tree).
# These values are the canonical OS-family identifiers used in BURN_PLATFORM
# enrollment and in per-tabtarget platform invariant assertions.
# BUBC_platforms_<family> tinder constants provide single source of truth so
# code refers to the identifier by family name rather than hardcoding the
# literal token at every comparison site.
BUBC_platforms_linux="bubep_linux"
BUBC_platforms_mac="bubep_mac"
BUBC_platforms_windows="bubep_windows"

# Windows OpenSSH layout — forward slashes throughout so identical strings
# work in PowerShell, terminal display, and icacls invocations.
BUBC_windows_sshd_config='C:/ProgramData/ssh/sshd_config'
BUBC_windows_admin_auth_keys='C:/ProgramData/ssh/administrators_authorized_keys'
BUBC_windows_ssh_port="22"
BUBC_windows_fw_rule_name="sshd"
BUBC_windows_fw_display_name="OpenSSH Server"

# eof

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BURN Regime - Bash Utility Regime Node Module
#
# BURN is a multi-instance regime — one file per node viceroyalty.
# Each instance lives at .buk/rbmn_nodes/<viceroyalty>/burn.env and is
# project-global (git-tracked, shared across station users).

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZBURN_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module burn multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBURN_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zburn_*)

zburn_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBURN_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module burn already kindled"

  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.

  buv_regime_enroll BURN

  buv_group_enroll "Node Identity"
  buv_string_enroll  BURN_HOST      1  253  "IP address or hostname of the remote node"
  buv_enum_enroll    BURN_PLATFORM  "OS family of the node (bubep_* identifier)"  bubep_linux bubep_mac bubep_windows

  # Guard against unexpected BURN_ variables not in enrollment
  buv_scope_sentinel BURN BURN_

  # Lock all enrolled BURN_ variables against mutation
  buv_lock BURN

  readonly ZBURN_KINDLED=1
}

zburn_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBURN_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module burn not kindled - call zburn_kindle first"
}

# Enforce all BURN enrollment validations
zburn_enforce() {
  zburn_sentinel

  buv_vet BURN
}

######################################################################
# Public Functions (burn_*)

# List available viceroyalty names as space-separated tokens.
# Emits one <viceroyalty>.${BUF_EXT_ALIAS} observation fact per profile
# (empty content; presence is the fact).
# Returns non-zero if no profiles are present.
# Prerequisite: BURD kindled (BURD_CONFIG_DIR)
burn_list_capture() {
  zburd_sentinel

  local -r z_nodes_dir="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/${BUBC_rbmn_nodes_subdir}"
  test -d "${z_nodes_dir}" || return 1

  local z_result=""
  local z_files=("${z_nodes_dir}"/*/burn.env)
  local z_i=""
  for z_i in "${!z_files[@]}"; do
    test -f "${z_files[$z_i]}" || continue
    local z_dir="${z_files[$z_i]%/*}"
    local z_viceroyalty="${z_dir##*/}"
    buf_write_fact_multi "${z_viceroyalty}" "${BUF_EXT_ALIAS}" ""
    z_result="${z_result}${z_result:+ }${z_viceroyalty}"
  done
  test -n "${z_result}" || return 1
  echo "${z_result}"
}

# Friendly-error die: emit "no folio supplied" message followed by available
# viceroyalties (or a "no profiles" hint when the directory is empty).
# Prerequisite: BURD kindled (BURD_CONFIG_DIR)
burn_die_no_folio() {
  zburd_sentinel
  local z_aliases=""
  if z_aliases=$(burn_list_capture 2>/dev/null); then
    buc_warn "BURN viceroyalty required as first argument."
    buc_step "Available viceroyalties under .buk/${BUBC_rbmn_nodes_subdir}/:"
    local z_v=""
    for z_v in ${z_aliases}; do
      buc_bare "        ${z_v}"
    done
  else
    buc_warn "BURN viceroyalty required as first argument."
    buc_step "No profiles found under .buk/${BUBC_rbmn_nodes_subdir}/."
    buc_bare "        Author one at .buk/${BUBC_rbmn_nodes_subdir}/<viceroyalty>/burn.env"
  fi
  buc_die "No BURN viceroyalty supplied."
}

# eof

38	  # Enroll all BURC variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering
39	
40	  buv_regime_enroll BURC

62	
63	  buv_group_enroll "Jurisdiction"
64	  buv_xname_enroll   BURC_WORKLOAD_USER         1   32  "Project-wide convention name for the workload OS user provisioned on every node"
65	
66	  # Guard against unexpected BURC_ variables not in enrollment
67	  buv_scope_sentinel BURC BURC_
68	
69	  # Export and lock all enrolled BURC_ variables — committed config,
70	  # needed by coordinator child processes across exec boundary
71	  buv_export_and_lock BURC
72	
73	  readonly ZBURC_KINDLED=1
74	}
75	
76	zburc_sentinel() {
77	  test "${ZBURC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module burc not kindled - call zburc_kindle first"
78	}
79	
80	# Enforce all BURC enrollment validations
81	zburc_enforce() {
82	  zburc_sentinel
83	
84	  buv_vet BURC
85	}
86	
87	# eof
88

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BURC Regime - Bash Utility Regime Configuration Module

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZBURC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module burc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBURC_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zburc_*)

zburc_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBURC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module burc already kindled"

  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.
  # Exception: BURC_BUK_DIR is derived from BURC_TOOLS_DIR at kindle time (before vet).
  readonly BURC_BUK_DIR="${BURC_TOOLS_DIR:-}/buk"

  # Enroll all BURC variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering

  buv_regime_enroll BURC

  buv_group_enroll "Station Reference"
  buv_string_enroll  BURC_STATION_FILE          1  512  "Path to developer's BURS station file"

  buv_group_enroll "Tabtarget Infrastructure"
  buv_string_enroll  BURC_TABTARGET_DIR         1  128  "Directory containing launcher scripts"
  buv_string_enroll  BURC_TABTARGET_DELIMITER   1    1  "Token separator in tabtarget filenames"

  buv_group_enroll "Project Structure"
  buv_string_enroll  BURC_TOOLS_DIR             1  128  "Directory containing tool scripts"
  buv_string_enroll  BURC_PROJECT_ROOT          1  512  "Path from burc.env to project root"
  buv_string_enroll  BURC_MANAGED_KITS          1  512  "Comma-separated kit list for vvx"

  buv_group_enroll "Build Output"
  buv_string_enroll  BURC_TEMP_ROOT_DIR         1  512  "Root directory for temporary files"
  buv_string_enroll  BURC_OUTPUT_ROOT_DIR       1  512  "Root directory for command output"

  buv_group_enroll "Logging"
  buv_xname_enroll   BURC_LOG_LAST              1   64  "Filename stem for last-run log"
  buv_xname_enroll   BURC_LOG_EXT               1   16  "Log file extension (without dot)"
  buv_string_enroll  BURC_BUK_DIR               1  256  "Derived: BUK directory"

  buv_group_enroll "Jurisdiction"
  buv_xname_enroll   BURC_WORKLOAD_USER         1   32  "Project-wide convention name for the workload OS user provisioned on every node"

  # Guard against unexpected BURC_ variables not in enrollment
  buv_scope_sentinel BURC BURC_

  # Export and lock all enrolled BURC_ variables — committed config,
  # needed by coordinator child processes across exec boundary
  buv_export_and_lock BURC

  readonly ZBURC_KINDLED=1
}

zburc_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBURC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module burc not kindled - call zburc_kindle first"
}

# Enforce all BURC enrollment validations
zburc_enforce() {
  zburc_sentinel

  buv_vet BURC
}

# eof

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# BUJB Jurisdiction Module - Implementation seat for fenestrate + garrison
20	#
21	# BCG-compliant module housing both BUS0 jurisdiction verbs (fenestrate,
22	# garrison) and the contracts not expressed in regime data:
23	# the three shell-letter -> command= directive mappings (b/c/w),
24	# the workload privkey destination paths on the remote per shell-letter,
25	# the canonical WSL distribution name, and the Windows OpenSSH sshd_config
26	# hardening directive set.
27	#
28	# Sub-letter b in bujb signals the bash-format implementation. A future
29	# PowerShell sibling would mint as bujp_jurisdiction.ps1.
30	
31	set -euo pipefail
32	
33	# Multiple inclusion detection
34	test -z "${ZBUJB_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module bujb multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
35	ZBUJB_SOURCED=1
36	
37	# Tinder constants (pure string literals — available at source time)
38	
39	# Shell-letter -> command= directive mappings.
40	# Forced commands routed through SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND keep workload account
41	# behaviour pinned to the chosen shell regardless of what the SSH client
42	# requests. Locked spec content; mirrored in BUSJG{B,C,W}.
43	BUJB_command_b='command="/bin/bash -lc \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding'
44	BUJB_command_c='command="C:/cygwin64/bin/bash --login -c \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding'
45	BUJB_command_w='command="C:/Windows/System32/wsl.exe --distribution rbtww-main --exec /bin/bash -lc \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding'
46	
47	# Shell-letter -> workload privkey destination path on the remote
48	# (relative to the workload account home directory).
49	BUJB_workload_keypath_b='.ssh/id_ed25519'
50	BUJB_workload_keypath_c='.ssh/id_ed25519'
51	BUJB_workload_keypath_w='.ssh/id_ed25519'
52	
53	# Canonical WSL distribution name reached by garrison-w.
54	BUJB_wsl_distribution='rbtww-main'
55	
56	# Windows OpenSSH sshd_config hardening directive set written by
57	# fenestrate phase 1. Newline-joined; each directive is asserted by
58	# bash-side parse after PowerShell Get-Content returns the raw bytes.
59	BUJB_sshd_hardening='PubkeyAuthentication yes
60	PasswordAuthentication no
61	PermitEmptyPasswords no'
62	
63	######################################################################
64	# Internal Functions (zbujb_*)
65	
66	zbujb_kindle() {
67	  test -z "${ZBUJB_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module bujb already kindled"
68	
69	  # Fenestrate temp file paths — ssh stdout/stderr captured here so
70	  # callers parse from disk (no `$(ssh ...)` capture).
71	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDOUT="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_phase1_stdout.txt"
72	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDERR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_phase1_stderr.txt"
73	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_RESTART_STDOUT="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_restart_stdout.txt"
74	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_RESTART_STDERR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_restart_stderr.txt"
75	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDOUT="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_phase2_stdout.txt"
76	  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDERR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_phase2_stderr.txt"
77	
78	  readonly ZBUJB_KINDLED=1
79	}
80	
81	zbujb_sentinel() {
82	  test "${ZBUJB_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module bujb not kindled - call zbujb_kindle first"
83	}
84	
85	# zbujb_check_key_file PATH VARNAME -- validate SSH private key at PATH:
86	# exists, mode 0600, parseable + unencrypted (ssh-keygen -y dry-load with
87	# empty passphrase). Diagnostic uses VARNAME as the field label.
88	zbujb_check_key_file() {
89	  local z_path="${1:-}"
90	  local z_varname="${2:-}"
91	
92	  test -n "${z_path}"   || buc_die "${z_varname}: empty path"
93	  test -f "${z_path}"   || buc_die "${z_varname}: file not found: ${z_path}"
94	
95	  local z_mode=""
96	  if z_mode=$(stat -f '%A' "${z_path}" 2>/dev/null); then
97	    : # macOS / BSD stat
98	  elif z_mode=$(stat -c '%a' "${z_path}" 2>/dev/null); then
99	    : # GNU/Linux stat
100	  else
101	    buc_die "${z_varname}: cannot stat ${z_path}"
102	  fi
103	  test "${z_mode}" = "600" || buc_die "${z_varname}: mode must be 0600, got ${z_mode}: ${z_path}"
104	
105	  ssh-keygen -y -P '' -f "${z_path}" >/dev/null 2>&1 \
106	    || buc_die "${z_varname}: ssh-keygen -y dry-load failed (passphrase-protected or malformed): ${z_path}"
107	}
108	
109	######################################################################
110	# Public Functions (bujb_*)
111	
112	# bujb_resolve_investiture -- sole runtime cross-validation entrypoint.
113	#
114	# Preconditions: BURC, BURN, BURP regimes already kindled and enforced
115	# (the bujb_cli furnish handles this for tabtarget callers; the cli's
116	# BURP and BURN sourcing also implicitly cross-validates that
117	# BURP_VICEROYALTY refers to a registered BURN profile by file presence).
118	#
119	# Behaviour: validates BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE and BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE
120	# (exist, mode 0600, ssh-keygen -y dry-load proves parseable + unencrypted) —
121	# the validation regime enforce cannot do because it requires running
122	# ssh-keygen against the private key. After validation, sets ZBUJB_RESOLVED=1
123	# as the I-have-been-validated sentinel. Callers reference the regime vars
124	# directly (BURP_*, BURN_*, BURC_*); no separate published namespace.
125	# Single-call-per-process; subsequent calls die.
126	bujb_resolve_investiture() {
127	  zbujb_sentinel
128	  zburc_sentinel
129	  zburn_sentinel
130	  zburp_sentinel
131	
132	  test -z "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" \
133	    || buc_die "bujb_resolve_investiture already called - single-call-per-process"
134	
135	  zbujb_check_key_file "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}" "BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE"
136	  zbujb_check_key_file "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"   "BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE"
137	
138	  readonly ZBUJB_RESOLVED=1
139	}
140	
141	# bujb_command_for SHELL_LETTER -- emit the workload authorized_keys
142	# command= directive for shell-letter b, c, or w (echoed to stdout).
143	bujb_command_for() {
144	  zbujb_sentinel
145	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
146	  case "${z_letter}" in
147	    b) echo "${BUJB_command_b}" ;;
148	    c) echo "${BUJB_command_c}" ;;
149	    w) echo "${BUJB_command_w}" ;;
150	    *) buc_die "bujb_command_for: invalid shell-letter (expected b/c/w): '${z_letter}'" ;;
151	  esac
152	}
153	
154	# bujb_workload_keypath_for SHELL_LETTER -- emit the workload privkey
155	# remote destination path (relative to workload home) for b, c, or w.
156	bujb_workload_keypath_for() {
157	  zbujb_sentinel
158	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
159	  case "${z_letter}" in
160	    b) echo "${BUJB_workload_keypath_b}" ;;
161	    c) echo "${BUJB_workload_keypath_c}" ;;
162	    w) echo "${BUJB_workload_keypath_w}" ;;
163	    *) buc_die "bujb_workload_keypath_for: invalid shell-letter (expected b/c/w): '${z_letter}'" ;;
164	  esac
165	}
166	
167	######################################################################
168	# Internal: Garrison helpers
169	
170	zbujb_assert_shell_letter() {
171	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
172	  case "${z_letter}" in
173	    b|c|w) ;;
174	    *) buc_die "Invalid shell-letter (expected b/c/w): '${z_letter}'" ;;
175	  esac
176	}
177	
178	# zbujb_garrison_assert_platform LETTER -- assert BURN_PLATFORM
179	# matches the shell-letter's required platform set.
180	zbujb_garrison_assert_platform() {
181	  zbujb_sentinel
182	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
183	  zbujb_assert_shell_letter "${z_letter}"
184	  case "${z_letter}" in
185	    b)
186	      case "${BURN_PLATFORM}" in
187	        bubep_linux|bubep_mac) ;;
188	        *) buc_die "garrison-b requires bubep_linux or bubep_mac, got '${BURN_PLATFORM}'" ;;
189	      esac
190	      ;;
191	    c|w)
192	      test "${BURN_PLATFORM}" = "bubep_windows" \
193	        || buc_die "garrison-${z_letter} requires bubep_windows, got '${BURN_PLATFORM}'"
194	      ;;
195	  esac
196	}
197	
198	# zbujb_workload_home LETTER -- echo the absolute workload home path on the
199	# remote node for the given shell-letter.
200	zbujb_workload_home() {
201	  zbujb_sentinel
202	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
203	  local z_wlu="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
204	  case "${z_letter}" in
205	    b)
206	      case "${BURN_PLATFORM}" in
207	        bubep_linux) echo "/home/${z_wlu}" ;;
208	        bubep_mac)   echo "/Users/${z_wlu}" ;;
209	        *)           buc_die "zbujb_workload_home: unsupported platform '${BURN_PLATFORM}'" ;;
210	      esac
211	      ;;
212	    c|w) echo "/home/${z_wlu}" ;;
213	    *)   buc_die "zbujb_workload_home: invalid letter '${z_letter}'" ;;
214	  esac
215	}
216	
217	# zbujb_admin_exec LETTER -- read a bash script from stdin and execute it on
218	# the remote node as the privileged user, wrapped in the shell-letter-
219	# appropriate invocation (native bash for b; Cygwin's bash --login for c;
220	# wsl.exe to BUJB_wsl_distribution as root for w).
221	zbujb_admin_exec() {
222	  zbujb_sentinel
223	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
224	  zbujb_assert_shell_letter "${z_letter}"
225	
226	  local z_remote_invoker
227	  case "${z_letter}" in
228	    b) z_remote_invoker='bash -s' ;;
229	    c) z_remote_invoker='C:/cygwin64/bin/bash --login -s' ;;
230	    w) z_remote_invoker="wsl.exe --distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} --user root bash -s" ;;
231	  esac
232	
233	  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"     \
234	      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                         \
235	      -o BatchMode=yes                              \
236	      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new           \
237	      -o ConnectTimeout=15                          \
238	      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}" \
239	      "${z_remote_invoker}"
240	}
241	
242	######################################################################
243	# Internal: Garrison steps (6-step ceremony per BUSJG{B,C,W})
244	
245	# Step 1 -- open admin SSH (test reachability under key-only auth).
246	zbujb_garrison_step1_admin_open() {
247	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
248	  buc_step "  [1/6] Open admin SSH (${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST})"
249	
250	  local z_exit=0
251	  zbujb_admin_exec "${z_letter}" <<<'exit 0' || z_exit=$?
252	  if test "${z_exit}" -ne 0; then
253	    case "${BURN_PLATFORM}" in
254	      bubep_windows)
255	        buc_die "Admin SSH failed (exit ${z_exit}). Run fenestrate first: tt/buw-jpF.Fenestrate.sh ${BURP_VICEROYALTY}"
256	        ;;
257	      *)
258	        buc_die "Admin SSH failed (exit ${z_exit}). Place admin pubkey via 'ssh-copy-id -i ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}.pub ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}' first."
259	        ;;
260	    esac
261	  fi
262	}
263	
264	# Step 2 -- destroy any existing workload account + home.
265	zbujb_garrison_step2_destroy() {
266	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
267	  local z_wlu="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
268	  local z_wlhome
269	  z_wlhome=$(zbujb_workload_home "${z_letter}")
270	  buc_step "  [2/6] Destroy workload (${z_wlu})"
271	
272	  case "${z_letter}" in
273	    b)
274	      zbujb_admin_exec b <<SCRIPT
275	set -uo pipefail
276	sudo -n userdel -r '${z_wlu}' 2>/dev/null || true
277	sudo -n rm -rf '${z_wlhome}' 2>/dev/null || true
278	SCRIPT
279	      ;;
280	    c)
281	      # Cygwin workload is a Windows local user; delete via net.exe then
282	      # purge the Cygwin home directory.
283	      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
284	set -uo pipefail
285	net.exe user '${z_wlu}' /delete > /dev/null 2>&1 || true
286	rm -rf '${z_wlhome}'                              2>/dev/null || true
287	rm -rf '/cygdrive/c/cygwin64/home/${z_wlu}'       2>/dev/null || true
288	SCRIPT
289	      ;;
290	    w)
291	      zbujb_admin_exec w <<SCRIPT
292	set -uo pipefail
293	userdel -r '${z_wlu}' 2>/dev/null || true
294	rm -rf '${z_wlhome}'  2>/dev/null || true
295	SCRIPT
296	      ;;
297	  esac
298	}
299	
300	# Step 3 -- create the workload account fresh, ssh-only, no privilege.
301	zbujb_garrison_step3_create() {
302	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
303	  local z_wlu="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
304	  buc_step "  [3/6] Create workload (${z_wlu})"
305	
306	  case "${z_letter}" in
307	    b)
308	      case "${BURN_PLATFORM}" in
309	        bubep_linux)
310	          zbujb_admin_exec b <<SCRIPT
311	set -euo pipefail
312	sudo -n useradd --create-home --shell /bin/bash '${z_wlu}'
313	sudo -n passwd  --lock '${z_wlu}'
314	SCRIPT
315	          ;;
316	        bubep_mac)
317	          # Mac uses dscl/sysadminctl; left for in-environment refinement.
318	          # Operator may need to seat a more idiomatic primary group ID.
319	          zbujb_admin_exec b <<SCRIPT
320	set -euo pipefail
321	sudo -n sysadminctl -addUser '${z_wlu}' -roleAccount
322	sudo -n dscl . -create '/Users/${z_wlu}' UserShell /bin/bash
323	SCRIPT
324	          ;;
325	      esac
326	      ;;
327	    c)
328	      # Cygwin reflects Windows user accounts; mint via net.exe with a
329	      # disabled-password posture (we want ssh-key-only).
330	      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
331	set -euo pipefail
332	net.exe user '${z_wlu}' /add /passwordreq:no /active:yes > /dev/null
333	mkpasswd -l -u '${z_wlu}' >> /etc/passwd
334	mkdir -p '/home/${z_wlu}'
335	chown -R '${z_wlu}' '/home/${z_wlu}'
336	SCRIPT
337	      ;;
338	    w)
339	      zbujb_admin_exec w <<SCRIPT
340	set -euo pipefail
341	useradd --create-home --shell /bin/bash '${z_wlu}'
342	passwd  --lock '${z_wlu}'
343	SCRIPT
344	      ;;
345	  esac
346	}
347	
348	# Step 4 -- write workload authorized_keys with the shell-letter command=
349	# directive and the workload pubkey (derived locally from the privkey).
350	zbujb_garrison_step4_place_trust() {
351	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
352	  local z_wlu="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
353	  local z_wlhome
354	  z_wlhome=$(zbujb_workload_home "${z_letter}")
355	  buc_step "  [4/6] Place workload trust (${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys)"
356	
357	  local z_command_directive
358	  z_command_directive=$(bujb_command_for "${z_letter}")
359	
360	  local z_pubkey
361	  z_pubkey=$(ssh-keygen -y -P '' -f "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}") \
362	    || buc_die "ssh-keygen -y failed for workload key: ${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"
363	
364	  local z_authkeys_line="${z_command_directive} ${z_pubkey}"
365	
366	  case "${z_letter}" in
367	    b|w)
368	      zbujb_admin_exec "${z_letter}" <<SCRIPT
369	set -euo pipefail
370	$([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")mkdir -p   '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
371	$([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")chmod 700  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
372	echo '${z_authkeys_line}' | $([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")tee '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys' > /dev/null
373	$([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")chmod 600  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys'
374	$([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")chown -R '${z_wlu}:${z_wlu}' '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
375	SCRIPT
376	      ;;
377	    c)
378	      # In Cygwin, file ownership/permissions interplay with NTFS ACLs.
379	      # mkdir/chmod/chown are Cygwin-mediated; run as the admin user
380	      # without sudo (Windows admins already have privilege).
381	      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
382	set -euo pipefail
383	mkdir -p   '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
384	chmod 700  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
385	echo '${z_authkeys_line}' > '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys'
386	chmod 600  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys'
387	chown -R '${z_wlu}' '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
388	SCRIPT
389	      ;;
390	  esac
391	}
392	
393	# Step 5 -- copy workload privkey to the remote at the shell-letter's
394	# hardcoded destination path, with workload ownership and 0600 mode.
395	zbujb_garrison_step5_plant_key() {
396	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
397	  local z_wlu="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
398	  local z_wlhome
399	  z_wlhome=$(zbujb_workload_home "${z_letter}")
400	  local z_keypath
401	  z_keypath=$(bujb_workload_keypath_for "${z_letter}")
402	  local z_target="${z_wlhome}/${z_keypath}"
403	  buc_step "  [5/6] Plant workload privkey (${z_target})"
404	
405	  local z_key_b64
406	  z_key_b64=$(base64 < "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}") \
407	    || buc_die "base64 encode failed for workload key: ${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"
408	
409	  case "${z_letter}" in
410	    b|w)
411	      local z_sudo=""
412	      test "${z_letter}" = "b" && z_sudo="sudo -n"
413	      zbujb_admin_exec "${z_letter}" <<SCRIPT
414	set -euo pipefail
415	ztmp=\$(mktemp)
416	trap 'rm -f "\${ztmp}"' EXIT
417	echo '${z_key_b64}' | base64 -d > "\${ztmp}"
418	${z_sudo} mkdir -p   '$(dirname "${z_target}")'
419	${z_sudo} install -m 600 -o '${z_wlu}' -g '${z_wlu}' "\${ztmp}" '${z_target}'
420	SCRIPT
421	      ;;
422	    c)
423	      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
424	set -euo pipefail
425	ztmp=\$(mktemp)
426	trap 'rm -f "\${ztmp}"' EXIT
427	echo '${z_key_b64}' | base64 -d > "\${ztmp}"
428	mkdir -p   '$(dirname "${z_target}")'
429	cp "\${ztmp}" '${z_target}'
430	chmod 600  '${z_target}'
431	chown      '${z_wlu}' '${z_target}'
432	SCRIPT
433	      ;;
434	  esac
435	}
436	
437	# Step 6 -- workload-side round-trip validation (knock).
438	zbujb_garrison_step6_validate() {
439	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
440	  buc_step "  [6/6] Validate workload round-trip"
441	
442	  local z_exit=0
443	  ssh -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
444	      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
445	      -o BatchMode=yes                            \
446	      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
447	      -o ConnectTimeout=10                        \
448	      "${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BURN_HOST}" \
449	      true                                        \
450	    || z_exit=$?
451	
452	  test "${z_exit}" -eq 0 \
453	    || buc_die "Workload round-trip failed (ssh exit ${z_exit}); the new account did not accept its own key."
454	}
455	
456	######################################################################
457	# Public: Garrison ceremony
458	
459	# bujb_garrison LETTER -- run the 6-step garrison ceremony for shell-letter
460	# b, c, or w. Caller must have invoked bujb_resolve_investiture beforehand.
461	bujb_garrison() {
462	  zbujb_sentinel
463	  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
464	    || buc_die "bujb_garrison: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"
465	
466	  local z_letter="${1:-}"
467	  zbujb_garrison_assert_platform "${z_letter}"
468	
469	  buc_step "Garrison-${z_letter}: ${BURP_VICEROYALTY} (${BURN_HOST})"
470	
471	  zbujb_garrison_step1_admin_open    "${z_letter}"
472	  zbujb_garrison_step2_destroy       "${z_letter}"
473	  zbujb_garrison_step3_create        "${z_letter}"
474	  zbujb_garrison_step4_place_trust   "${z_letter}"
475	  zbujb_garrison_step5_plant_key     "${z_letter}"
476	  zbujb_garrison_step6_validate      "${z_letter}"
477	
478	  buc_step "Garrison-${z_letter} succeeded"
479	}
480	
481	######################################################################
482	# Internal: Fenestrate helpers (BUSJPF — Windows OpenSSH only)
483	#
484	# Each phase 1 chunk is a separate ssh call with publickey,password preferred
485	# auth. On a fresh node, chunk A's first publickey attempt fails and ssh
486	# falls through to /dev/tty password prompt — operator types once. After
487	# chunk A places the admin pubkey, chunk B's publickey attempt succeeds
488	# (the running sshd's old config still permits pubkey auth and now reads
489	# the updated authorized_keys), so no further prompt. Phase 2 is key-only.
490	# No ControlMaster, no traps, no 2>/dev/null.
491	
492	zbujb_fenestrate_assert_platform() {
493	  zbujb_sentinel
494	  test "${BURN_PLATFORM}" = "bubep_windows" \
495	    || buc_die "fenestrate requires bubep_windows, got '${BURN_PLATFORM}'"
496	}
497	
498	# zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback STDOUT_FILE STDERR_FILE
499	# Reads a PowerShell script from stdin and runs it on the remote node as
500	# the privileged user. publickey,password preferred (BatchMode=no allows
501	# /dev/tty password prompt on first run). Default Windows OpenSSH shell is
502	# cmd.exe; we explicitly invoke `powershell -NoProfile -File -` to feed
503	# the script via stdin. Returns ssh's exit code (caller decides).
504	zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback() {
505	  zbujb_sentinel
506	  local -r z_stdout_file="${1:-}"
507	  local -r z_stderr_file="${2:-}"
508	  test -n "${z_stdout_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback: stdout_file required"
509	  test -n "${z_stderr_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback: stderr_file required"
510	
511	  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"            \
512	      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                                \
513	      -o BatchMode=no                                      \
514	      -o PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password       \
515	      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new                  \
516	      -o ConnectTimeout=15                                 \
517	      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}" \
518	      'powershell -NoProfile -File -'                      \
519	      > "${z_stdout_file}"                                 \
520	      2> "${z_stderr_file}"
521	}
522	
523	# zbujb_fenestrate_exec_keyonly STDOUT_FILE STDERR_FILE
524	# Same as above but BatchMode=yes (no password fallback). Used for phase 2.
525	zbujb_fenestrate_exec_keyonly() {
526	  zbujb_sentinel
527	  local -r z_stdout_file="${1:-}"
528	  local -r z_stderr_file="${2:-}"
529	  test -n "${z_stdout_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_exec_keyonly: stdout_file required"
530	  test -n "${z_stderr_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_exec_keyonly: stderr_file required"
531	
532	  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"            \
533	      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                                \
534	      -o BatchMode=yes                                     \
535	      -o PreferredAuthentications=publickey                \
536	      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new                  \
537	      -o ConnectTimeout=15                                 \
538	      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}" \
539	      'powershell -NoProfile -File -'                      \
540	      > "${z_stdout_file}"                                 \
541	      2> "${z_stderr_file}"
542	}
543	
544	# zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives REMOTE_FILE -- load REMOTE_FILE (raw
545	# sshd_config bytes from PowerShell Get-Content), strip CR, then assert
546	# each directive in BUJB_sshd_hardening appears with the expected value.
547	# Load-then-iterate (no nested while-read on stdin); pure parameter
548	# expansion + case (no awk/grep/tr).
549	zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives() {
550	  zbujb_sentinel
551	  local -r z_remote_file="${1:-}"
552	  test -n "${z_remote_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives: remote_file required"
553	  test -f "${z_remote_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives: remote_file not found: ${z_remote_file}"
554	
555	  local -r z_raw_bytes=$(<"${z_remote_file}")
556	  test -n "${z_raw_bytes}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives: empty remote sshd_config bytes: ${z_remote_file}"
557	  local -r z_clean_bytes="${z_raw_bytes//$'\r'/}"
558	
559	  local z_directives_roll=()
560	  local z_pair=""
561	  while IFS= read -r z_pair || test -n "${z_pair}"; do
562	    test -n "${z_pair}" || continue
563	    z_directives_roll+=("${z_pair}")
564	  done <<<"${BUJB_sshd_hardening}"
565	
566	  local z_remote_lines_roll=()
567	  local z_line=""
568	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
569	    z_remote_lines_roll+=("${z_line}")
570	  done <<<"${z_clean_bytes}"
571	
572	  local z_directive=""
573	  local z_expected=""
574	  local z_actual=""
575	  local z_after_directive=""
576	  local z_i=0
577	  local z_j=0
578	  for z_i in "${!z_directives_roll[@]}"; do
579	    z_directive="${z_directives_roll[$z_i]%% *}"
580	    z_expected="${z_directives_roll[$z_i]#* }"
581	    z_actual=""
582	
583	    for z_j in "${!z_remote_lines_roll[@]}"; do
584	      case "${z_remote_lines_roll[$z_j]}" in
585	        "${z_directive} "*)
586	          z_after_directive="${z_remote_lines_roll[$z_j]#"${z_directive} "}"
587	          z_actual="${z_after_directive%% *}"
588	          break
589	          ;;
590	      esac
591	    done
592	
593	    test -n "${z_actual}" \
594	      || buc_die "Hardening verify: directive '${z_directive}' missing or commented in remote sshd_config"
595	    test "${z_actual}" = "${z_expected}" \
596	      || buc_die "Hardening verify: ${z_directive}: expected '${z_expected}', got '${z_actual}'"
597	  done
598	}
599	
600	######################################################################
601	# Internal: Fenestrate phases
602	
603	# zbujb_fenestrate_phase1 -- chunk A (install admin pubkey idempotently +
604	# icacls + merge sshd_config hardening + sshd -t + emit raw bytes); bash-
605	# side parse + verify; then chunk B (Restart-Service sshd, disconnect
606	# expected — exit code ignored).
607	zbujb_fenestrate_phase1() {
608	  zbujb_sentinel
609	
610	  local z_pubkey=""
611	  z_pubkey=$(ssh-keygen -y -P '' -f "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}") \
612	    || buc_die "ssh-keygen -y failed for admin key: ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"
613	
614	  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Chunk A: pubkey + icacls + sshd_config + sshd -t + emit (operator may be prompted for admin password on first run)"
615	
616	  zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback \
617	      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDOUT}"      \
618	      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDERR}"      \
619	    <<PS1 || buc_die "Phase 1 chunk A failed — admin pubkey/icacls/sshd_config/sshd -t did not all succeed; see ${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDERR}"
620	\$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
621	
622	\$pubkey = '${z_pubkey}'
623	\$adminAuthKeys = "\$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\administrators_authorized_keys"
624	\$sshConfig    = "\$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\sshd_config"
625	
626	# Idempotent admin pubkey install
627	if (-not (Test-Path \$adminAuthKeys)) {
628	  New-Item -Path \$adminAuthKeys -ItemType File -Force | Out-Null
629	}
630	\$existingLines = @(Get-Content \$adminAuthKeys -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)
631	if (\$existingLines -notcontains \$pubkey) {
632	  Add-Content -Path \$adminAuthKeys -Value \$pubkey -Encoding ASCII
633	}
634	
635	# icacls lockdown (idempotent)
636	icacls \$adminAuthKeys /inheritance:r /grant 'SYSTEM:F' /grant 'BUILTIN\\Administrators:F' 2>&1 | Out-Null
637	if (\$LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { throw "icacls failed (exit \$LASTEXITCODE)" }
638	
639	# Idempotent sshd_config harden — replace the first matching line for
640	# each directive (commented or otherwise) and drop subsequent duplicates;
641	# append directives that are absent. Convergent on re-run.
642	\$directives = [ordered]@{
643	  'PubkeyAuthentication'   = 'yes'
644	  'PasswordAuthentication' = 'no'
645	  'PermitEmptyPasswords'   = 'no'
646	}
647	
648	\$lines = @(Get-Content \$sshConfig)
649	\$out   = New-Object System.Collections.Generic.List[string]
650	\$seen  = @{}
651	foreach (\$line in \$lines) {
652	  \$matched = \$false
653	  foreach (\$k in \$directives.Keys) {
654	    if (\$line -match "^\\s*#*\\s*\$k\\s+\\S") {
655	      if (-not \$seen.ContainsKey(\$k)) {
656	        \$out.Add("\$k \$(\$directives[\$k])")
657	        \$seen[\$k] = \$true
658	      }
659	      \$matched = \$true
660	      break
661	    }
662	  }
663	  if (-not \$matched) { \$out.Add(\$line) }
664	}
665	foreach (\$k in \$directives.Keys) {
666	  if (-not \$seen.ContainsKey(\$k)) { \$out.Add("\$k \$(\$directives[\$k])") }
667	}
668	Set-Content -Path \$sshConfig -Value \$out -Encoding ASCII
669	
670	# sshd -t — penultimate atomic op; aborts before restart on bad config
671	& 'C:\\Windows\\System32\\OpenSSH\\sshd.exe' -t
672	if (\$LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { throw "sshd -t failed (exit \$LASTEXITCODE)" }
673	
674	# Emit raw bytes for bash-side parse (last op so output is clean)
675	Get-Content \$sshConfig -Raw
676	PS1
677	
678	  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Verify hardened directives via bash-side parse"
679	  zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDOUT}"
680	
681	  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Chunk B: Restart-Service sshd (disconnect expected — exit code ignored)"
682	  zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback \
683	      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_RESTART_STDOUT}"     \
684	      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_RESTART_STDERR}"     \
685	    <<'PS1' || true
686	$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue'
687	Restart-Service sshd
688	PS1
689	}
690	
691	# zbujb_fenestrate_phase2 -- reconnect via key-only auth and re-verify the
692	# hardened directives served by the running sshd.
693	zbujb_fenestrate_phase2() {
694	  zbujb_sentinel
695	
696	  buc_step "  [Phase 2] Reconnect under key-only auth + re-verify"
697	
698	  # Allow sshd a moment to come back from Restart-Service.
699	  sleep 3
700	
701	  zbujb_fenestrate_exec_keyonly                 \
702	      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDOUT}"       \
703	      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDERR}"       \
704	    <<'PS1' || buc_die "Phase 2 reconnect failed — possible brick: admin pubkey not honored after restart or sshd did not come back up; see ${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDERR}"
705	$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
706	Get-Content "$env:ProgramData\ssh\sshd_config" -Raw
707	PS1
708	
709	  zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDOUT}"
710	}
711	
712	######################################################################
713	# Public: Fenestrate ceremony
714	
715	# bujb_fenestrate -- run the two-phase fenestrate ceremony for a Windows
716	# OpenSSH node. Caller must have invoked bujb_resolve_investiture beforehand.
717	bujb_fenestrate() {
718	  zbujb_sentinel
719	  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
720	    || buc_die "bujb_fenestrate: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"
721	
722	  zbujb_fenestrate_assert_platform
723	
724	  buc_step "Fenestrate: ${BURP_VICEROYALTY} (${BURN_HOST})"
725	
726	  zbujb_fenestrate_phase1
727	  zbujb_fenestrate_phase2
728	
729	  buc_step "Fenestrate succeeded"
730	}
731	
732	# eof
733

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUJB Jurisdiction Module - Implementation seat for fenestrate + garrison
#
# BCG-compliant module housing both BUS0 jurisdiction verbs (fenestrate,
# garrison) and the contracts not expressed in regime data:
# the three shell-letter -> command= directive mappings (b/c/w),
# the workload privkey destination paths on the remote per shell-letter,
# the canonical WSL distribution name, and the Windows OpenSSH sshd_config
# hardening directive set.
#
# Sub-letter b in bujb signals the bash-format implementation. A future
# PowerShell sibling would mint as bujp_jurisdiction.ps1.

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZBUJB_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module bujb multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBUJB_SOURCED=1

# Tinder constants (pure string literals — available at source time)

# Shell-letter -> command= directive mappings.
# Forced commands routed through SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND keep workload account
# behaviour pinned to the chosen shell regardless of what the SSH client
# requests. Locked spec content; mirrored in BUSJG{B,C,W}.
BUJB_command_b='command="/bin/bash -lc \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding'
BUJB_command_c='command="C:/cygwin64/bin/bash --login -c \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding'
BUJB_command_w='command="C:/Windows/System32/wsl.exe --distribution rbtww-main --exec /bin/bash -lc \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding'

# Shell-letter -> workload privkey destination path on the remote
# (relative to the workload account home directory).
BUJB_workload_keypath_b='.ssh/id_ed25519'
BUJB_workload_keypath_c='.ssh/id_ed25519'
BUJB_workload_keypath_w='.ssh/id_ed25519'

# Canonical WSL distribution name reached by garrison-w.
BUJB_wsl_distribution='rbtww-main'

# Windows OpenSSH sshd_config hardening directive set written by
# fenestrate phase 1. Newline-joined; each directive is asserted by
# bash-side parse after PowerShell Get-Content returns the raw bytes.
BUJB_sshd_hardening='PubkeyAuthentication yes
PasswordAuthentication no
PermitEmptyPasswords no'

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zbujb_*)

zbujb_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBUJB_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module bujb already kindled"

  # Fenestrate temp file paths — ssh stdout/stderr captured here so
  # callers parse from disk (no `$(ssh ...)` capture).
  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDOUT="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_phase1_stdout.txt"
  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDERR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_phase1_stderr.txt"
  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_RESTART_STDOUT="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_restart_stdout.txt"
  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_RESTART_STDERR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_restart_stderr.txt"
  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDOUT="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_phase2_stdout.txt"
  readonly ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDERR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_fenestrate_phase2_stderr.txt"

  readonly ZBUJB_KINDLED=1
}

zbujb_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBUJB_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module bujb not kindled - call zbujb_kindle first"
}

# zbujb_check_key_file PATH VARNAME -- validate SSH private key at PATH:
# exists, mode 0600, parseable + unencrypted (ssh-keygen -y dry-load with
# empty passphrase). Diagnostic uses VARNAME as the field label.
zbujb_check_key_file() {
  local z_path="${1:-}"
  local z_varname="${2:-}"

  test -n "${z_path}"   || buc_die "${z_varname}: empty path"
  test -f "${z_path}"   || buc_die "${z_varname}: file not found: ${z_path}"

  local z_mode=""
  if z_mode=$(stat -f '%A' "${z_path}" 2>/dev/null); then
    : # macOS / BSD stat
  elif z_mode=$(stat -c '%a' "${z_path}" 2>/dev/null); then
    : # GNU/Linux stat
  else
    buc_die "${z_varname}: cannot stat ${z_path}"
  fi
  test "${z_mode}" = "600" || buc_die "${z_varname}: mode must be 0600, got ${z_mode}: ${z_path}"

  ssh-keygen -y -P '' -f "${z_path}" >/dev/null 2>&1 \
    || buc_die "${z_varname}: ssh-keygen -y dry-load failed (passphrase-protected or malformed): ${z_path}"
}

######################################################################
# Public Functions (bujb_*)

# bujb_resolve_investiture -- sole runtime cross-validation entrypoint.
#
# Preconditions: BURC, BURN, BURP regimes already kindled and enforced
# (the bujb_cli furnish handles this for tabtarget callers; the cli's
# BURP and BURN sourcing also implicitly cross-validates that
# BURP_VICEROYALTY refers to a registered BURN profile by file presence).
#
# Behaviour: validates BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE and BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE
# (exist, mode 0600, ssh-keygen -y dry-load proves parseable + unencrypted) —
# the validation regime enforce cannot do because it requires running
# ssh-keygen against the private key. After validation, sets ZBUJB_RESOLVED=1
# as the I-have-been-validated sentinel. Callers reference the regime vars
# directly (BURP_*, BURN_*, BURC_*); no separate published namespace.
# Single-call-per-process; subsequent calls die.
bujb_resolve_investiture() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  zburc_sentinel
  zburn_sentinel
  zburp_sentinel

  test -z "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" \
    || buc_die "bujb_resolve_investiture already called - single-call-per-process"

  zbujb_check_key_file "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}" "BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE"
  zbujb_check_key_file "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"   "BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE"

  readonly ZBUJB_RESOLVED=1
}

# bujb_command_for SHELL_LETTER -- emit the workload authorized_keys
# command= directive for shell-letter b, c, or w (echoed to stdout).
bujb_command_for() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  case "${z_letter}" in
    b) echo "${BUJB_command_b}" ;;
    c) echo "${BUJB_command_c}" ;;
    w) echo "${BUJB_command_w}" ;;
    *) buc_die "bujb_command_for: invalid shell-letter (expected b/c/w): '${z_letter}'" ;;
  esac
}

# bujb_workload_keypath_for SHELL_LETTER -- emit the workload privkey
# remote destination path (relative to workload home) for b, c, or w.
bujb_workload_keypath_for() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  case "${z_letter}" in
    b) echo "${BUJB_workload_keypath_b}" ;;
    c) echo "${BUJB_workload_keypath_c}" ;;
    w) echo "${BUJB_workload_keypath_w}" ;;
    *) buc_die "bujb_workload_keypath_for: invalid shell-letter (expected b/c/w): '${z_letter}'" ;;
  esac
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Garrison helpers

zbujb_assert_shell_letter() {
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  case "${z_letter}" in
    b|c|w) ;;
    *) buc_die "Invalid shell-letter (expected b/c/w): '${z_letter}'" ;;
  esac
}

# zbujb_garrison_assert_platform LETTER -- assert BURN_PLATFORM
# matches the shell-letter's required platform set.
zbujb_garrison_assert_platform() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  zbujb_assert_shell_letter "${z_letter}"
  case "${z_letter}" in
    b)
      case "${BURN_PLATFORM}" in
        bubep_linux|bubep_mac) ;;
        *) buc_die "garrison-b requires bubep_linux or bubep_mac, got '${BURN_PLATFORM}'" ;;
      esac
      ;;
    c|w)
      test "${BURN_PLATFORM}" = "bubep_windows" \
        || buc_die "garrison-${z_letter} requires bubep_windows, got '${BURN_PLATFORM}'"
      ;;
  esac
}

# zbujb_workload_home LETTER -- echo the absolute workload home path on the
# remote node for the given shell-letter.
zbujb_workload_home() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  local z_wlu="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
  case "${z_letter}" in
    b)
      case "${BURN_PLATFORM}" in
        bubep_linux) echo "/home/${z_wlu}" ;;
        bubep_mac)   echo "/Users/${z_wlu}" ;;
        *)           buc_die "zbujb_workload_home: unsupported platform '${BURN_PLATFORM}'" ;;
      esac
      ;;
    c|w) echo "/home/${z_wlu}" ;;
    *)   buc_die "zbujb_workload_home: invalid letter '${z_letter}'" ;;
  esac
}

# zbujb_admin_exec LETTER -- read a bash script from stdin and execute it on
# the remote node as the privileged user, wrapped in the shell-letter-
# appropriate invocation (native bash for b; Cygwin's bash --login for c;
# wsl.exe to BUJB_wsl_distribution as root for w).
zbujb_admin_exec() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  zbujb_assert_shell_letter "${z_letter}"

  local z_remote_invoker
  case "${z_letter}" in
    b) z_remote_invoker='bash -s' ;;
    c) z_remote_invoker='C:/cygwin64/bin/bash --login -s' ;;
    w) z_remote_invoker="wsl.exe --distribution ${BUJB_wsl_distribution} --user root bash -s" ;;
  esac

  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"     \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                         \
      -o BatchMode=yes                              \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new           \
      -o ConnectTimeout=15                          \
      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}" \
      "${z_remote_invoker}"
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Garrison steps (6-step ceremony per BUSJG{B,C,W})

# Step 1 -- open admin SSH (test reachability under key-only auth).
zbujb_garrison_step1_admin_open() {
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  buc_step "  [1/6] Open admin SSH (${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST})"

  local z_exit=0
  zbujb_admin_exec "${z_letter}" <<<'exit 0' || z_exit=$?
  if test "${z_exit}" -ne 0; then
    case "${BURN_PLATFORM}" in
      bubep_windows)
        buc_die "Admin SSH failed (exit ${z_exit}). Run fenestrate first: tt/buw-jpF.Fenestrate.sh ${BURP_VICEROYALTY}"
        ;;
      *)
        buc_die "Admin SSH failed (exit ${z_exit}). Place admin pubkey via 'ssh-copy-id -i ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}.pub ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}' first."
        ;;
    esac
  fi
}

# Step 2 -- destroy any existing workload account + home.
zbujb_garrison_step2_destroy() {
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  local z_wlu="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
  local z_wlhome
  z_wlhome=$(zbujb_workload_home "${z_letter}")
  buc_step "  [2/6] Destroy workload (${z_wlu})"

  case "${z_letter}" in
    b)
      zbujb_admin_exec b <<SCRIPT
set -uo pipefail
sudo -n userdel -r '${z_wlu}' 2>/dev/null || true
sudo -n rm -rf '${z_wlhome}' 2>/dev/null || true
SCRIPT
      ;;
    c)
      # Cygwin workload is a Windows local user; delete via net.exe then
      # purge the Cygwin home directory.
      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
set -uo pipefail
net.exe user '${z_wlu}' /delete > /dev/null 2>&1 || true
rm -rf '${z_wlhome}'                              2>/dev/null || true
rm -rf '/cygdrive/c/cygwin64/home/${z_wlu}'       2>/dev/null || true
SCRIPT
      ;;
    w)
      zbujb_admin_exec w <<SCRIPT
set -uo pipefail
userdel -r '${z_wlu}' 2>/dev/null || true
rm -rf '${z_wlhome}'  2>/dev/null || true
SCRIPT
      ;;
  esac
}

# Step 3 -- create the workload account fresh, ssh-only, no privilege.
zbujb_garrison_step3_create() {
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  local z_wlu="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
  buc_step "  [3/6] Create workload (${z_wlu})"

  case "${z_letter}" in
    b)
      case "${BURN_PLATFORM}" in
        bubep_linux)
          zbujb_admin_exec b <<SCRIPT
set -euo pipefail
sudo -n useradd --create-home --shell /bin/bash '${z_wlu}'
sudo -n passwd  --lock '${z_wlu}'
SCRIPT
          ;;
        bubep_mac)
          # Mac uses dscl/sysadminctl; left for in-environment refinement.
          # Operator may need to seat a more idiomatic primary group ID.
          zbujb_admin_exec b <<SCRIPT
set -euo pipefail
sudo -n sysadminctl -addUser '${z_wlu}' -roleAccount
sudo -n dscl . -create '/Users/${z_wlu}' UserShell /bin/bash
SCRIPT
          ;;
      esac
      ;;
    c)
      # Cygwin reflects Windows user accounts; mint via net.exe with a
      # disabled-password posture (we want ssh-key-only).
      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
set -euo pipefail
net.exe user '${z_wlu}' /add /passwordreq:no /active:yes > /dev/null
mkpasswd -l -u '${z_wlu}' >> /etc/passwd
mkdir -p '/home/${z_wlu}'
chown -R '${z_wlu}' '/home/${z_wlu}'
SCRIPT
      ;;
    w)
      zbujb_admin_exec w <<SCRIPT
set -euo pipefail
useradd --create-home --shell /bin/bash '${z_wlu}'
passwd  --lock '${z_wlu}'
SCRIPT
      ;;
  esac
}

# Step 4 -- write workload authorized_keys with the shell-letter command=
# directive and the workload pubkey (derived locally from the privkey).
zbujb_garrison_step4_place_trust() {
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  local z_wlu="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
  local z_wlhome
  z_wlhome=$(zbujb_workload_home "${z_letter}")
  buc_step "  [4/6] Place workload trust (${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys)"

  local z_command_directive
  z_command_directive=$(bujb_command_for "${z_letter}")

  local z_pubkey
  z_pubkey=$(ssh-keygen -y -P '' -f "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "ssh-keygen -y failed for workload key: ${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"

  local z_authkeys_line="${z_command_directive} ${z_pubkey}"

  case "${z_letter}" in
    b|w)
      zbujb_admin_exec "${z_letter}" <<SCRIPT
set -euo pipefail
$([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")mkdir -p   '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
$([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")chmod 700  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
echo '${z_authkeys_line}' | $([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")tee '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys' > /dev/null
$([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")chmod 600  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys'
$([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")chown -R '${z_wlu}:${z_wlu}' '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
SCRIPT
      ;;
    c)
      # In Cygwin, file ownership/permissions interplay with NTFS ACLs.
      # mkdir/chmod/chown are Cygwin-mediated; run as the admin user
      # without sudo (Windows admins already have privilege).
      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
set -euo pipefail
mkdir -p   '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
chmod 700  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
echo '${z_authkeys_line}' > '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys'
chmod 600  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys'
chown -R '${z_wlu}' '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
SCRIPT
      ;;
  esac
}

# Step 5 -- copy workload privkey to the remote at the shell-letter's
# hardcoded destination path, with workload ownership and 0600 mode.
zbujb_garrison_step5_plant_key() {
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  local z_wlu="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
  local z_wlhome
  z_wlhome=$(zbujb_workload_home "${z_letter}")
  local z_keypath
  z_keypath=$(bujb_workload_keypath_for "${z_letter}")
  local z_target="${z_wlhome}/${z_keypath}"
  buc_step "  [5/6] Plant workload privkey (${z_target})"

  local z_key_b64
  z_key_b64=$(base64 < "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "base64 encode failed for workload key: ${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"

  case "${z_letter}" in
    b|w)
      local z_sudo=""
      test "${z_letter}" = "b" && z_sudo="sudo -n"
      zbujb_admin_exec "${z_letter}" <<SCRIPT
set -euo pipefail
ztmp=\$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "\${ztmp}"' EXIT
echo '${z_key_b64}' | base64 -d > "\${ztmp}"
${z_sudo} mkdir -p   '$(dirname "${z_target}")'
${z_sudo} install -m 600 -o '${z_wlu}' -g '${z_wlu}' "\${ztmp}" '${z_target}'
SCRIPT
      ;;
    c)
      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
set -euo pipefail
ztmp=\$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "\${ztmp}"' EXIT
echo '${z_key_b64}' | base64 -d > "\${ztmp}"
mkdir -p   '$(dirname "${z_target}")'
cp "\${ztmp}" '${z_target}'
chmod 600  '${z_target}'
chown      '${z_wlu}' '${z_target}'
SCRIPT
      ;;
  esac
}

# Step 6 -- workload-side round-trip validation (knock).
zbujb_garrison_step6_validate() {
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  buc_step "  [6/6] Validate workload round-trip"

  local z_exit=0
  ssh -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
      -o BatchMode=yes                            \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
      -o ConnectTimeout=10                        \
      "${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BURN_HOST}" \
      true                                        \
    || z_exit=$?

  test "${z_exit}" -eq 0 \
    || buc_die "Workload round-trip failed (ssh exit ${z_exit}); the new account did not accept its own key."
}

######################################################################
# Public: Garrison ceremony

# bujb_garrison LETTER -- run the 6-step garrison ceremony for shell-letter
# b, c, or w. Caller must have invoked bujb_resolve_investiture beforehand.
bujb_garrison() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
    || buc_die "bujb_garrison: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"

  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  zbujb_garrison_assert_platform "${z_letter}"

  buc_step "Garrison-${z_letter}: ${BURP_VICEROYALTY} (${BURN_HOST})"

  zbujb_garrison_step1_admin_open    "${z_letter}"
  zbujb_garrison_step2_destroy       "${z_letter}"
  zbujb_garrison_step3_create        "${z_letter}"
  zbujb_garrison_step4_place_trust   "${z_letter}"
  zbujb_garrison_step5_plant_key     "${z_letter}"
  zbujb_garrison_step6_validate      "${z_letter}"

  buc_step "Garrison-${z_letter} succeeded"
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Fenestrate helpers (BUSJPF — Windows OpenSSH only)
#
# Each phase 1 chunk is a separate ssh call with publickey,password preferred
# auth. On a fresh node, chunk A's first publickey attempt fails and ssh
# falls through to /dev/tty password prompt — operator types once. After
# chunk A places the admin pubkey, chunk B's publickey attempt succeeds
# (the running sshd's old config still permits pubkey auth and now reads
# the updated authorized_keys), so no further prompt. Phase 2 is key-only.
# No ControlMaster, no traps, no 2>/dev/null.

zbujb_fenestrate_assert_platform() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${BURN_PLATFORM}" = "bubep_windows" \
    || buc_die "fenestrate requires bubep_windows, got '${BURN_PLATFORM}'"
}

# zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback STDOUT_FILE STDERR_FILE
# Reads a PowerShell script from stdin and runs it on the remote node as
# the privileged user. publickey,password preferred (BatchMode=no allows
# /dev/tty password prompt on first run). Default Windows OpenSSH shell is
# cmd.exe; we explicitly invoke `powershell -NoProfile -File -` to feed
# the script via stdin. Returns ssh's exit code (caller decides).
zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  local -r z_stdout_file="${1:-}"
  local -r z_stderr_file="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_stdout_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback: stdout_file required"
  test -n "${z_stderr_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback: stderr_file required"

  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"            \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                                \
      -o BatchMode=no                                      \
      -o PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password       \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new                  \
      -o ConnectTimeout=15                                 \
      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}" \
      'powershell -NoProfile -File -'                      \
      > "${z_stdout_file}"                                 \
      2> "${z_stderr_file}"
}

# zbujb_fenestrate_exec_keyonly STDOUT_FILE STDERR_FILE
# Same as above but BatchMode=yes (no password fallback). Used for phase 2.
zbujb_fenestrate_exec_keyonly() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  local -r z_stdout_file="${1:-}"
  local -r z_stderr_file="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_stdout_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_exec_keyonly: stdout_file required"
  test -n "${z_stderr_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_exec_keyonly: stderr_file required"

  ssh -i "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"            \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                                \
      -o BatchMode=yes                                     \
      -o PreferredAuthentications=publickey                \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new                  \
      -o ConnectTimeout=15                                 \
      "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}" \
      'powershell -NoProfile -File -'                      \
      > "${z_stdout_file}"                                 \
      2> "${z_stderr_file}"
}

# zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives REMOTE_FILE -- load REMOTE_FILE (raw
# sshd_config bytes from PowerShell Get-Content), strip CR, then assert
# each directive in BUJB_sshd_hardening appears with the expected value.
# Load-then-iterate (no nested while-read on stdin); pure parameter
# expansion + case (no awk/grep/tr).
zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  local -r z_remote_file="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_remote_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives: remote_file required"
  test -f "${z_remote_file}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives: remote_file not found: ${z_remote_file}"

  local -r z_raw_bytes=$(<"${z_remote_file}")
  test -n "${z_raw_bytes}" || buc_die "zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives: empty remote sshd_config bytes: ${z_remote_file}"
  local -r z_clean_bytes="${z_raw_bytes//$'\r'/}"

  local z_directives_roll=()
  local z_pair=""
  while IFS= read -r z_pair || test -n "${z_pair}"; do
    test -n "${z_pair}" || continue
    z_directives_roll+=("${z_pair}")
  done <<<"${BUJB_sshd_hardening}"

  local z_remote_lines_roll=()
  local z_line=""
  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    z_remote_lines_roll+=("${z_line}")
  done <<<"${z_clean_bytes}"

  local z_directive=""
  local z_expected=""
  local z_actual=""
  local z_after_directive=""
  local z_i=0
  local z_j=0
  for z_i in "${!z_directives_roll[@]}"; do
    z_directive="${z_directives_roll[$z_i]%% *}"
    z_expected="${z_directives_roll[$z_i]#* }"
    z_actual=""

    for z_j in "${!z_remote_lines_roll[@]}"; do
      case "${z_remote_lines_roll[$z_j]}" in
        "${z_directive} "*)
          z_after_directive="${z_remote_lines_roll[$z_j]#"${z_directive} "}"
          z_actual="${z_after_directive%% *}"
          break
          ;;
      esac
    done

    test -n "${z_actual}" \
      || buc_die "Hardening verify: directive '${z_directive}' missing or commented in remote sshd_config"
    test "${z_actual}" = "${z_expected}" \
      || buc_die "Hardening verify: ${z_directive}: expected '${z_expected}', got '${z_actual}'"
  done
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Fenestrate phases

# zbujb_fenestrate_phase1 -- chunk A (install admin pubkey idempotently +
# icacls + merge sshd_config hardening + sshd -t + emit raw bytes); bash-
# side parse + verify; then chunk B (Restart-Service sshd, disconnect
# expected — exit code ignored).
zbujb_fenestrate_phase1() {
  zbujb_sentinel

  local z_pubkey=""
  z_pubkey=$(ssh-keygen -y -P '' -f "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "ssh-keygen -y failed for admin key: ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"

  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Chunk A: pubkey + icacls + sshd_config + sshd -t + emit (operator may be prompted for admin password on first run)"

  zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback \
      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDOUT}"      \
      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDERR}"      \
    <<PS1 || buc_die "Phase 1 chunk A failed — admin pubkey/icacls/sshd_config/sshd -t did not all succeed; see ${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDERR}"
\$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'

\$pubkey = '${z_pubkey}'
\$adminAuthKeys = "\$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\administrators_authorized_keys"
\$sshConfig    = "\$env:ProgramData\\ssh\\sshd_config"

# Idempotent admin pubkey install
if (-not (Test-Path \$adminAuthKeys)) {
  New-Item -Path \$adminAuthKeys -ItemType File -Force | Out-Null
}
\$existingLines = @(Get-Content \$adminAuthKeys -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)
if (\$existingLines -notcontains \$pubkey) {
  Add-Content -Path \$adminAuthKeys -Value \$pubkey -Encoding ASCII
}

# icacls lockdown (idempotent)
icacls \$adminAuthKeys /inheritance:r /grant 'SYSTEM:F' /grant 'BUILTIN\\Administrators:F' 2>&1 | Out-Null
if (\$LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { throw "icacls failed (exit \$LASTEXITCODE)" }

# Idempotent sshd_config harden — replace the first matching line for
# each directive (commented or otherwise) and drop subsequent duplicates;
# append directives that are absent. Convergent on re-run.
\$directives = [ordered]@{
  'PubkeyAuthentication'   = 'yes'
  'PasswordAuthentication' = 'no'
  'PermitEmptyPasswords'   = 'no'
}

\$lines = @(Get-Content \$sshConfig)
\$out   = New-Object System.Collections.Generic.List[string]
\$seen  = @{}
foreach (\$line in \$lines) {
  \$matched = \$false
  foreach (\$k in \$directives.Keys) {
    if (\$line -match "^\\s*#*\\s*\$k\\s+\\S") {
      if (-not \$seen.ContainsKey(\$k)) {
        \$out.Add("\$k \$(\$directives[\$k])")
        \$seen[\$k] = \$true
      }
      \$matched = \$true
      break
    }
  }
  if (-not \$matched) { \$out.Add(\$line) }
}
foreach (\$k in \$directives.Keys) {
  if (-not \$seen.ContainsKey(\$k)) { \$out.Add("\$k \$(\$directives[\$k])") }
}
Set-Content -Path \$sshConfig -Value \$out -Encoding ASCII

# sshd -t — penultimate atomic op; aborts before restart on bad config
& 'C:\\Windows\\System32\\OpenSSH\\sshd.exe' -t
if (\$LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { throw "sshd -t failed (exit \$LASTEXITCODE)" }

# Emit raw bytes for bash-side parse (last op so output is clean)
Get-Content \$sshConfig -Raw
PS1

  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Verify hardened directives via bash-side parse"
  zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE1_STDOUT}"

  buc_step "  [Phase 1] Chunk B: Restart-Service sshd (disconnect expected — exit code ignored)"
  zbujb_fenestrate_exec_with_password_fallback \
      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_RESTART_STDOUT}"     \
      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_RESTART_STDERR}"     \
    <<'PS1' || true
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue'
Restart-Service sshd
PS1
}

# zbujb_fenestrate_phase2 -- reconnect via key-only auth and re-verify the
# hardened directives served by the running sshd.
zbujb_fenestrate_phase2() {
  zbujb_sentinel

  buc_step "  [Phase 2] Reconnect under key-only auth + re-verify"

  # Allow sshd a moment to come back from Restart-Service.
  sleep 3

  zbujb_fenestrate_exec_keyonly                 \
      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDOUT}"       \
      "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDERR}"       \
    <<'PS1' || buc_die "Phase 2 reconnect failed — possible brick: admin pubkey not honored after restart or sshd did not come back up; see ${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDERR}"
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
Get-Content "$env:ProgramData\ssh\sshd_config" -Raw
PS1

  zbujb_fenestrate_verify_directives "${ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_PHASE2_STDOUT}"
}

######################################################################
# Public: Fenestrate ceremony

# bujb_fenestrate -- run the two-phase fenestrate ceremony for a Windows
# OpenSSH node. Caller must have invoked bujb_resolve_investiture beforehand.
bujb_fenestrate() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
    || buc_die "bujb_fenestrate: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"

  zbujb_fenestrate_assert_platform

  buc_step "Fenestrate: ${BURP_VICEROYALTY} (${BURN_HOST})"

  zbujb_fenestrate_phase1
  zbujb_fenestrate_phase2

  buc_step "Fenestrate succeeded"
}

# eof

lastPrompt: mount A-    Review pace ₢A-AAm "implement-jurisdiction" against its docket and paddock.   Do NOT wrap, do NOT make code changes. Read-only review with a written   findings report at the end.    ## Wha…

18	#
19	# BUJB CLI - Command line interface for the jurisdiction module
20	#
21	# Furnish loads BURC, BURS, BURP (per investiture folio), and the cross-
22	# referenced BURN profile, then kindles bujb. Command functions call
23	# bujb_resolve_investiture before doing any work; per-verb platform
24	# invariants are asserted inline at the command body.
25	
26	set -euo pipefail
27	
28	source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
29	
30	######################################################################
31	# Command Functions
32	
33	# bujb_resolve - diagnostic: load investiture and print resolved fields.
34	# No remote action; verifies regime + key-file health for a target.
35	bujb_resolve() {
36	  buc_doc_brief "Cross-validate an investiture and display the regime fields it resolves"
37	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
38	
39	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
40	
41	  bujb_resolve_investiture
42	
43	  buc_step "Resolved investiture ${BUZ_FOLIO}:"
44	  buc_bare "  BURP_VICEROYALTY        = ${BURP_VICEROYALTY}"
45	  buc_bare "  BURN_HOST               = ${BURN_HOST}"
46	  buc_bare "  BURN_PLATFORM           = ${BURN_PLATFORM}"
47	  buc_bare "  BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER    = ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}"
48	  buc_bare "  BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE= ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}"
49	  buc_bare "  BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE  = ${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"
50	  buc_bare "  BURC_WORKLOAD_USER      = ${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
51	}
52	
53	# bujb_knock - probe workload reachability (workload SSH + remote no-op).
54	bujb_knock() {
55	  buc_doc_brief "Probe workload SSH reachability for an investiture"
56	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
57

59

61	
62	  buc_step "Knocking ${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BURN_HOST} (${BURP_VICEROYALTY})"
63	
64	  ssh -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
65	      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
66	      -o BatchMode=yes                            \
67	      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
68	      -o ConnectTimeout=10                        \

72	
73	  buc_step "Knock succeeded"
74	}

76	# bujb_command_file - stream a local command file's contents to the workload
77	# remote shell. Captures stdout/stderr/exit to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR. Per spec:
78	# returns 0 when dispatch + capture complete (independent of remote exit);
79	# non-zero on SSH-level failure (exit 255).
80	bujb_command_file() {
81	  buc_doc_brief "Execute a local command file as workload; capture stdout/stderr/exit"
82	  buc_doc_param "command_file" "Local file streamed as the remote command body"
83	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
84	
85	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
86	
87	  local z_command_file="${1:-}"
88	  test -n "${z_command_file}"   || buc_usage_die
89	  test -f "${z_command_file}"   || buc_die "Command file not found: ${z_command_file}"
90	  test -r "${z_command_file}"   || buc_die "Command file not readable: ${z_command_file}"
91	
92	  bujb_resolve_investiture
93	
94	  buc_step "Streaming ${z_command_file} to ${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BURN_HOST}"
95	  buc_step "Output dir: ${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}"
96	
97	  local z_exit=0
98	  ssh -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
99	      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
100	      -o BatchMode=yes                            \
101	      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
102	      -o ConnectTimeout=10                        \
103	      "${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BURN_HOST}" \
104	      'bash -s'                                   \
105	      < "${z_command_file}"                       \
106	      > "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/stdout.log"           \
107	      2> "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/stderr.log"          \
108	    || z_exit=$?
109	
110	  echo "${z_exit}" > "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/exitcode"
111	
112	  test "${z_exit}" -ne 255 \
113	    || buc_die "SSH connection or authentication failed (exit 255). See ${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/stderr.log"
114	
115	  buc_step "Remote exit code: ${z_exit}"
116	  buc_step "Capture complete"
117	}
118	
119	# bujb_fenestrate - fenestrate ceremony (Windows OpenSSH only).
120	bujb_fenestrate_command() {

122	  buc_doc_shown || return 0

125	  bujb_resolve_investiture
126	  bujb_fenestrate
127	}
128

130	bujb_garrison_bash() {

132	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
133	
134	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
135	  bujb_resolve_investiture

137	}
138	
139	# bujb_garrison_cygwin - garrison ceremony, Cygwin bash workload shell (Windows).

142	  buc_doc_shown || return 0

148	
149	# bujb_garrison_wsl - garrison ceremony, WSL bash workload shell (Windows).
150	bujb_garrison_wsl() {

152	  buc_doc_shown || return 0

154	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
155	  bujb_resolve_investiture

158	
159	# bujb_interactive_session - hand control to ssh as workload.
160	bujb_interactive_session() {
161	  buc_doc_brief "Interactive SSH session as workload"
162	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
163	
164	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || burp_die_no_folio
165	
166	  bujb_resolve_investiture

169	
170	  exec ssh -t                                     \
171	      -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
172	      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
173	      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \

177	
178	######################################################################
179	# Furnish and Main

182	  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
183	  buc_doc_env "BURD_CONFIG_DIR       " "BUK config directory (dispatch-provided)"
184	  buc_doc_env_done || return 0
