### Prefer Bash Builtins Over External Tools

```bash
# ✅ String manipulation with parameter expansion
"${z_path##*/}"                    # Instead of: basename
"${z_path%/*}"                     # Instead of: dirname
"${z_string//old/new}"             # Instead of: sed 's/old/new/g'
"${z_string#prefix}"               # Instead of: sed 's/^prefix//'
"${z_string%suffix}"               # Instead of: sed 's/suffix$//'

# ✅ Reading lines with 'read' — guard handles missing trailing newline
while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
  echo "Processing: ${z_line}" || buc_die "Failed to echo line"
done < "${Z_MODULE_INPUT_FILE}"

# ✅ Arithmetic with $((...))
z_result=$((z_value * 2 + 10))    # Instead of: expr or bc
```

### Temp Files Instead of Command Substitution

All external command output capture uses temp files. The three prohibited `$()` patterns are: `local z_var=$(cmd)` (local swallows exit status), pipelines inside `$()` (hidden intermediate failures), and unguarded `$()` (silent failure). The only `$()` permitted on functions is for `_capture` (secrets that must not touch disk) and `_recite` (read-only roll access) — both with explicit `|| buc_die` or `|| return 1`. Bash introspection (`compgen`, `declare -F`, `mktemp`) is also permitted in `$()` as no file-based alternative exists.

```bash
# ❌ Anti-pattern: nested command substitution
z_result=$(echo $(complex_command) | process)  # Hidden failures!

# ❌ Anti-pattern: unguarded command substitution
z_timestamp=$(date +%s)  # Failure is silent!

# ✅ Correct: temp files make failures visible afterward
complex_command > "${Z_MODULE_TEMP1}" || buc_die "Complex command failed"
process < "${Z_MODULE_TEMP1}" > "${Z_MODULE_TEMP2}" || buc_die "Process failed"
read -r z_result < "${Z_MODULE_TEMP2}" || buc_die "No result"

# ✅ Correct: single external command via temp file
date +%s > "${Z_MODULE_TEMP1}" || buc_die "Failed to get timestamp"
local -r z_timestamp=$(<"${Z_MODULE_TEMP1}")
test -n "${z_timestamp}" || buc_die "Empty timestamp"

# ⚠️ EXCEPTION: Secrets use _capture functions, never temp files
# ⚠️ EXCEPTION: _recite functions return via stdout (read-only roll access)
```

**Temp file lifecycle**: Temp files under `BURD_TEMP_DIR` are **preserved after execution** for forensic debugging. Never delete temp files in module code — their persistence is intentional. Cleanup is handled by infrastructure outside BCG's scope.

### Stderr Capture — Never Suppress

Never use `2>/dev/null` on external commands. Redirect stderr to a temp file so forensic evidence is preserved when commands fail. Include the stderr file path in the `buc_die` message so the user can inspect it.

```bash
# ❌ Anti-pattern: stderr suppressed — failure cause invisible
curl -sS "${z_url}" -o "${z_output_file}" 2>/dev/null \
  || buc_die "Failed to fetch ${z_url}"

# ✅ Correct: stderr captured to temp file, referenced in die message
curl -sS "${z_url}" -o "${z_output_file}" 2>"${z_stderr_file}" \
  || buc_die "Failed to fetch ${z_url} — see ${z_stderr_file}"
```

**Stderr file naming**: Use a kindle `_PREFIX` constant (grouped with other temp file prefixes for collision visibility) and append a discriminator at the usage site.

**In loops**, use an auto-incrementing integer for uniqueness rather than trusting loop data values to be unique:

```bash
# In kindle — grouped with sibling temp file prefixes
Z«PREFIX»_«COMMAND»_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/«prefix»_«command»_"

# In command function — integer discriminator ensures uniqueness
local z_index=0
while IFS='|' read -r z_varname z_image z_tag || test -n "${z_varname}"; do
  local z_manifest_file="${Z«PREFIX»_«COMMAND»_PREFIX}${z_index}_manifest.json"
  local z_stderr_file="${Z«PREFIX»_«COMMAND»_PREFIX}${z_index}_inspect_stderr.txt"

  docker manifest inspect "${z_image}:${z_tag}" \
    > "${z_manifest_file}" 2>"${z_stderr_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to inspect ${z_image}:${z_tag} — see ${z_stderr_file}"

  z_index=$((z_index + 1))

content.text:
I've now read BCG carefully. Quoting the operative passages, then producing both repair plans.

## BCG citations (load-bearing for both questions)

**Line 502 (Temp Files Instead of Command Substitution):**

> All external command output capture uses temp files. The three prohibited `$()` patterns are: `local z_var=$(cmd)` (local swallows exit status), pipelines inside `$()` (hidden intermediate failures), and unguarded `$()` (silent failure). The only `$()` permitted on functions is for `_capture` (secrets that must not touch disk) and `_recite` (read-only roll access) — both with explicit `|| buc_die` or `|| return 1`. Bash introspection (`compgen`, `declare -F`, `mktemp`) is also permitted in `$()` as no file-based alternative exists.

**Line 529 (Stderr Capture — Never Suppress):**

> Never use `2>/dev/null` on external commands. Redirect stderr to a temp file so forensic evidence is preserved when commands fail. Include the stderr file path in the `buc_die` message so the user can inspect it.

## Answer to Q2: is `$(stat …)` inherently BCG-incompatible?

**Yes.** Unambiguously. `stat` is an external command, not bash introspection (`compgen`/`declare -F`/`mktemp`) and not a `_capture`/`_recite` function. BCG line 502 forbids `$()` capture from externals categorically. The fact that the line *also* carries `2>/dev/null` is a second, independent BCG violation per line 529. So `zbujb_check_key_file` lines 96 + 98 each carry **two** BCG violations stacked on the same statement.

This also widened my view of the AAm BCG audit. Re-reading line 502 against the docket review-axis wording I was given ("`ssh`, `base64`, `ssh-keygen` exception sites only") — **BCG itself does not grant ssh/base64/ssh-keygen as exception sites.** Those words appear nowhere in BCG. The exemptions BCG grants are `_capture`/`_recite`/bash-introspection. So the AAm bujb actually has **more** capture-from-external violations than my first pass surfaced. Full inventory below.

## Full BCG capture/stderr inventory in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` (post-AAm)

| Line | Pattern | Violation |
|------|---------|-----------|
| 96 | `if z_mode=$(stat -f '%A' "${z_path}" 2>/dev/null); then` | `$()` external capture + stderr suppression |
| 98 | `elif z_mode=$(stat -c '%a' "${z_path}" 2>/dev/null); then` | `$()` external capture + stderr suppression |
| 105 | `ssh-keygen -y -P '' -f "${z_path}" >/dev/null 2>&1` | stderr suppression |
| 361 | `z_pubkey=$(ssh-keygen -y -P '' -f "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}")` | `$()` external capture |
| 406 | `z_key_b64=$(base64 < "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}")` | `$()` external capture |
| 611 | `z_pubkey=$(ssh-keygen -y -P '' -f "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}")` | `$()` external capture |

(Internal-function captures at lines 269/354/358/399/401 — `$(zbujb_workload_home …)`, `$(bujb_command_for …)`, `$(bujb_workload_keypath_for …)` — are also BCG-prohibited per line 502, since none of those are `_capture`/`_recite` functions. Surfacing as a related class but suggesting they ride a separate, larger refactor — see scope note at end.)

---

## Repair Plan #1 — `zbujb_check_key_file` (covers stat + ssh-keygen drylod)

### Kindle additions (`zbujb_kindle`)

Add these `readonly Z*` paths grouped with the existing `ZBUJB_FENESTRATE_*` block:

```bash
readonly ZBUJB_STAT_STDOUT_PRIV="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_stat_priv_stdout.txt"
readonly ZBUJB_STAT_STDERR_PRIV="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_stat_priv_stderr.txt"
readonly ZBUJB_STAT_STDOUT_WORK="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_stat_work_stdout.txt"
readonly ZBUJB_STAT_STDERR_WORK="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_stat_work_stderr.txt"
readonly ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDOUT_PRIV="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_dryload_priv_stdout.txt"
readonly ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDERR_PRIV="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_dryload_priv_stderr.txt"
readonly ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDOUT_WORK="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_dryload_work_stdout.txt"
readonly ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDERR_WORK="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_dryload_work_stderr.txt"
```

Per-key (`PRIV` vs `WORK`) discriminators rather than a shared file or an integer-suffix loop pattern: there are exactly two callers, statically known, so the discriminator-suffix style from BCG line 547 is overkill.

### Function signature change

`zbujb_check_key_file` currently takes `(PATH, VARNAME)` and uses VARNAME both for diagnostics and (implicitly) to discriminate caller. It now needs to know **which temp-file pair** to use. Cleanest: take a third parameter — a shorthand discriminator (`priv` or `work`) — and route stdout/stderr file pairs by it.

Or, equivalently, two small wrapper functions (`zbujb_check_priv_key_file`, `zbujb_check_work_key_file`) each calling a shared `_impl` with bound paths. I lean toward the parameter approach (less duplication).

### Rewritten body (proposed)

```bash
# zbujb_check_key_file PATH VARNAME SLOT -- validate SSH private key:
# exists, mode 0600, parseable + unencrypted (ssh-keygen -y dry-load).
# SLOT ∈ {priv, work} selects the temp-file pair for stat + dry-load capture.
zbujb_check_key_file() {
  local z_path="${1:-}"
  local z_varname="${2:-}"
  local z_slot="${3:-}"

  test -n "${z_path}"    || buc_die "${z_varname}: empty path"
  test -f "${z_path}"    || buc_die "${z_varname}: file not found: ${z_path}"

  local z_stat_stdout="" z_stat_stderr="" z_dry_stdout="" z_dry_stderr=""
  case "${z_slot}" in
    priv)
      z_stat_stdout="${ZBUJB_STAT_STDOUT_PRIV}"
      z_stat_stderr="${ZBUJB_STAT_STDERR_PRIV}"
      z_dry_stdout="${ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDOUT_PRIV}"
      z_dry_stderr="${ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDERR_PRIV}"
      ;;
    work)
      z_stat_stdout="${ZBUJB_STAT_STDOUT_WORK}"
      z_stat_stderr="${ZBUJB_STAT_STDERR_WORK}"
      z_dry_stdout="${ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDOUT_WORK}"
      z_dry_stderr="${ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDERR_WORK}"
      ;;
    *)
      buc_die "zbujb_check_key_file: invalid slot (expected priv|work): '${z_slot}'"
      ;;
  esac

  # BSD stat first, GNU stat fallback. Stderr to per-flavor temp files for
  # forensics; the failed flavor's stderr is expected on the wrong platform.
  if stat -f '%A' "${z_path}" > "${z_stat_stdout}" 2> "${z_stat_stderr}"; then
    : # macOS / BSD stat succeeded
  elif stat -c '%a' "${z_path}" > "${z_stat_stdout}" 2> "${z_stat_stderr}"; then
    : # GNU/Linux stat succeeded (overwrites BSD attempt's stderr)
  else
    buc_die "${z_varname}: cannot stat ${z_path} — see ${z_stat_stderr}"
  fi

  local z_mode
  z_mode=$(<"${z_stat_stdout}")               # bash builtin $(<file) — permitted
  z_mode="${z_mode//$'\n'/}"                  # trim newline
  test "${z_mode}" = "600" \
    || buc_die "${z_varname}: mode must be 0600, got ${z_mode}: ${z_path}"

  ssh-keygen -y -P '' -f "${z_path}" \
      > "${z_dry_stdout}" \
      2> "${z_dry_stderr}" \
    || buc_die "${z_varname}: ssh-keygen -y dry-load failed (passphrase-protected or malformed): ${z_path} — see ${z_dry_stderr}"
}
```

Call-site update at `bujb_resolve_investiture`:
```bash
zbujb_check_key_file "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}" "BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE" priv
zbujb_check_key_file "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"   "BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE"   work
```

### Notes on the BSD-then-GNU dance

The fallback pattern is preserved because the *local station* platform isn't carried in any kindled regime variable (BURN_PLATFORM describes the remote node). One alternative is to detect once at kindle via `uname > "${ZBUJB_UNAME_STDOUT}" 2> "${ZBUJB_UNAME_STDERR}"` and store `ZBUJB_STAT_FLAVOR=bsd|gnu` — but `uname` is itself an external requiring the same temp-file dance, so the savings are illusory and the indirection is harder to read than two-line BSD-then-GNU. Keeping the inline fallback.

The per-flavor stderr file overwrite-on-second-attempt is OK because forensic value is "what was the *final* failure cause" — when both stats fail, the GNU stderr is what matters; when BSD fails but GNU succeeds, the BSD stderr is uninteresting. If forensic value is wanted from the failed-then-recovered case, two stderr files (`_BSD_STDERR`, `_GNU_STDERR`) are easy.

---

## Repair Plan #2 — `ssh-keygen` capture (lines 361, 611) and `base64` capture (line 406)

These are larger surgeries because the captured stdout (pubkey, base64-encoded privkey) currently composes inline into a constructed string or heredoc. Plan:

### Lines 361 + 611 (ssh-keygen stdout = workload/admin pubkey)

Add to kindle:
```bash
readonly ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDOUT_WORK="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_pubkey_work.txt"
readonly ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDERR_WORK="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_pubkey_work_stderr.txt"
readonly ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDOUT_PRIV="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_pubkey_priv.txt"
readonly ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDERR_PRIV="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_pubkey_priv_stderr.txt"
```

At line ~361 (step 4 place workload trust):
```bash
ssh-keygen -y -P '' -f "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}" \
    > "${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDOUT_WORK}" \
    2> "${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDERR_WORK}" \
  || buc_die "ssh-keygen -y failed for workload key: ${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE} — see ${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDERR_WORK}"
local z_pubkey
z_pubkey=$(<"${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDOUT_WORK}")
z_pubkey="${z_pubkey//$'\n'/}"   # ssh-keygen adds trailing newline
```

At line ~611 (fenestrate phase 1, admin pubkey for installation):
```bash
ssh-keygen -y -P '' -f "${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}" \
    > "${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDOUT_PRIV}" \
    2> "${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDERR_PRIV}" \
  || buc_die "ssh-keygen -y failed for admin key: ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE} — see ${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDERR_PRIV}"
local z_pubkey
z_pubkey=$(<"${ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDOUT_PRIV}")
z_pubkey="${z_pubkey//$'\n'/}"
```

### Line 406 (base64 stdout = b64-encoded workload privkey)

Add to kindle:
```bash
readonly ZBUJB_KEY_B64_STDOUT="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_key_b64.txt"
readonly ZBUJB_KEY_B64_STDERR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bujb_key_b64_stderr.txt"
```

At line ~406:
```bash
base64 < "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}" \
    > "${ZBUJB_KEY_B64_STDOUT}" \
    2> "${ZBUJB_KEY_B64_STDERR}" \
  || buc_die "base64 encode failed for workload key: ${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE} — see ${ZBUJB_KEY_B64_STDERR}"
local z_key_b64
z_key_b64=$(<"${ZBUJB_KEY_B64_STDOUT}")
z_key_b64="${z_key_b64//$'\n'/}"
```

### Line 105 (ssh-keygen stderr suppression in zbujb_check_key_file)

Already covered by Plan #1's dry-load rewrite — no separate fix needed.

---

## Repair Plan #3 — BUS0 + BUSJG{B,C,W} `command=` literal lock-in

The docket bullet says the literals are spec content. Implementation has them in the module only. The repair makes spec the source of truth and the module the mirror.

### Where to put the three literal strings

Two reasonable placements, each with tradeoff:

**Option A — per-spec inline at step 4.** Each of BUSJGB/BUSJGC/BUSJGW grows a literal `command="…"` string in step 4 ("Place workload trust"), with a one-line note that `bujb_jurisdiction.sh:BUJB_command_<letter>` mirrors the spec.
- *Pro:* literal sits adjacent to its consumer (the step that places it).
- *Con:* three separate definition sites; if a rev tweaks the directive structure, three edits.

**Option B — single aggregate table in BUS0.** Add a new linked-term subsection in `[[bujb_jurisdiction]]` (BUS0:1498) listing all three letters in one table. BUSJGB/BUSJGC/BUSJGW step 4 references the BUS0 table by linked term.
- *Pro:* single source-of-truth in spec; one edit point for cross-letter changes.
- *Con:* spec readers of BUSJGC need to chase a cross-spec reference to see the literal.

**Recommendation: Option B.** The three strings share a contract (forced command via `SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND`, identical no-* hardening tail). The shape commonality is more visible aggregated. BUSJG{B,C,W} retain a one-line reference: "the shell-letter `c` `command=` directive specified in {bujb_jurisdiction} (workload command directives table)". The letter-distinguishing detail (the invoker path) is what differs and what's worth aggregating.

### Concrete BUS0 edit (Option B)

Inside `[[bujb_jurisdiction]] {bujb_jurisdiction}::` (BUS0:1498-1521), add a `+` continuation block introducing a literal-block table. Sketch:

```adoc
+
The three workload `command=` directives written by {busn_garrison} into the
workload account's authorized_keys are locked spec content; the module
mirrors them at `BUJB_command_{b,c,w}`.

[%header]
|===
| Shell-letter | `command=` directive

| `b` (native bash, Linux/Mac)
| `command="/bin/bash -lc \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding`

| `c` (Cygwin bash, Windows)
| `command="C:/cygwin64/bin/bash --login -c \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding`

| `w` (WSL bash, Windows)
| `command="C:/Windows/System32/wsl.exe --distribution rbtww-main --exec /bin/bash -lc \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding`
|===
```

(Exact AsciiDoc table syntax may need backtick escaping inside cells; the module file uses single-quoted bash literals which avoid most spec-render headaches, but inside an AsciiDoc table cell the `|` and backtick chars need attention. Working that out is part of the implementation pace.)

### BUSJGB / BUSJGC / BUSJGW step 4 update

Step 4 prose stays narrative but the trailing `from {bujb_jurisdiction}` becomes a sharper pointer:

> *Place workload trust*: write the workload pubkey (derived via `ssh-keygen -y`) to the workload's authorized_keys with the shell-letter `b` `command=` directive **specified in the {bujb_jurisdiction} workload-command-directives table** and mirrored at `BUJB_command_b`.

(Same pattern with `c` and `w` for the other two specs.)

### Module-side comment update

`Tools/buk/bujb_jurisdiction.sh:39-45` — the comment block above `BUJB_command_b/c/w` already says "Locked spec content; mirrored in BUSJG{B,C,W}." That needs to flip direction to point at BUS0:

```
# Shell-letter -> command= directive mappings.
# Locked spec content lives in BUS0 §Remote Node Access, [[bujb_jurisdiction]]
# linked-term definition (workload command directives table). These constants
# mirror that spec; any change must land in BUS0 first.
```

### What to NOT lock in spec

The other module hardcodes are already adequately specified:
- `BUJB_workload_keypath_*` paths — referenced in BUSJG{B,C,W} step 5 as "destination path hardcoded in `{bujb_jurisdiction}`"; spec already acknowledges module ownership. Could be locked too for symmetry but the docket bullet only calls out `command=` strings.
- `BUJB_wsl_distribution = rbtww-main` — already locked in BUS0:1515 and BUSJGW:5.
- `BUJB_sshd_hardening` directives — already locked in BUSJPF step 4 (lines 58-61).

Recommendation: only lock the `command=` directives per docket scope; defer keypath/wsl-distro symmetry consideration as a separate paddock-level question (it's a reasonable follow-up, but the docket didn't call for it).

---

## Repair Plan #4 — per-verb platform assertion location

Per your direction: **leave as is**. The per-verb assertion helpers (`zbujb_fenestrate_assert_platform`, `zbujb_garrison_assert_platform`) called from inside the public verb function are functionally equivalent to inline-at-tabtarget and arguably clearer. The docket bullet's "(2-line check; helper stays generic)" wording will need a minor narrative update at next paddock review, but no source change.

---

## Scope note for AAm wrap

These three repair plans, taken together, form a coherent **L3f BCG cleanup + L4 spec-lock** delta on top of AAm. Three honest options for where they land:

1. **Fold into AAm** as a new pace before wrap (₢A-AAn). Preserves AAm's "this is the spec → code → spec round-trip done" framing. Risk: scope creep relative to docket.
2. **Defer to a fresh post-AAm pace** (₢A-AAo or whatever) named e.g. "bcg-tighten-bujb-and-spec-lock-command-directives". Cleanest docket discipline.
3. **Split**: BCG repair into AAm (it's a documented L3 verification finding); spec lock-in into a fresh pace (it's a structural docket-vs-source disagreement that's bigger than wrap-time scope).

The internal-function `$()` captures (lines 269/354/358/399/401 — `zbujb_workload_home`, `bujb_command_for`, `bujb_workload_keypath_for`) are also BCG line-502 violations but require renaming those functions to `_capture` style or refactoring to yawp pattern — bigger refactor that probably warrants its own paddock conversation rather than slipping into AAm. Surfacing as a finding for AAn or a follow-up heat.

No edits made. Awaiting your call on (1) which repairs to fold into wrap vs. defer, and (2) whether to broaden the BCG sweep to the internal-function captures now or later.

input.old_string:
# zbujb_check_key_file PATH VARNAME -- validate SSH private key at PATH:
# exists, mode 0600, parseable + unencrypted (ssh-keygen -y dry-load with
# empty passphrase). Diagnostic uses VARNAME as the field label.
zbujb_check_key_file() {
  local z_path="${1:-}"
  local z_varname="${2:-}"

  test -n "${z_path}"   || buc_die "${z_varname}: empty path"
  test -f "${z_path}"   || buc_die "${z_varname}: file not found: ${z_path}"

  local z_mode=""
  if z_mode=$(stat -f '%A' "${z_path}" 2>/dev/null); then
    : # macOS / BSD stat
  elif z_mode=$(stat -c '%a' "${z_path}" 2>/dev/null); then
    : # GNU/Linux stat
  else
    buc_die "${z_varname}: cannot stat ${z_path}"
  fi
  test "${z_mode}" = "600" || buc_die "${z_varname}: mode must be 0600, got ${z_mode}: ${z_path}"

  ssh-keygen -y -P '' -f "${z_path}" >/dev/null 2>&1 \
    || buc_die "${z_varname}: ssh-keygen -y dry-load failed (passphrase-protected or malformed): ${z_path}"
}
input.new_string:
# zbujb_check_key_file PATH VARNAME SLOT -- validate SSH private key at PATH:
# exists, mode 0600, parseable + unencrypted (ssh-keygen -y dry-load with
# empty passphrase). Diagnostic uses VARNAME as the field label. SLOT
# (priv|work) selects the kindle temp-file pair used for stat + dry-load
# stdout/stderr capture so failures preserve forensic evidence per BCG.
zbujb_check_key_file() {
  local z_path="${1:-}"
  local z_varname="${2:-}"
  local z_slot="${3:-}"

  test -n "${z_path}"    || buc_die "${z_varname}: empty path"
  test -f "${z_path}"    || buc_die "${z_varname}: file not found: ${z_path}"

  local z_stat_stdout="" z_stat_stderr="" z_dry_stdout="" z_dry_stderr=""
  case "${z_slot}" in
    priv)
      z_stat_stdout="${ZBUJB_STAT_STDOUT_PRIV}"
      z_stat_stderr="${ZBUJB_STAT_STDERR_PRIV}"
      z_dry_stdout="${ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDOUT_PRIV}"
      z_dry_stderr="${ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDERR_PRIV}"
      ;;
    work)
      z_stat_stdout="${ZBUJB_STAT_STDOUT_WORK}"
      z_stat_stderr="${ZBUJB_STAT_STDERR_WORK}"
      z_dry_stdout="${ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDOUT_WORK}"
      z_dry_stderr="${ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDERR_WORK}"
      ;;
    *)
      buc_die "zbujb_check_key_file: invalid slot (expected priv|work): '${z_slot}'"
      ;;
  esac

  # BSD stat first (Mac), GNU stat fallback (Linux). The failed flavor's
  # stderr is overwritten by the successful flavor's invocation; on total
  # failure z_stat_stderr holds the GNU-attempt error.
  if stat -f '%A' "${z_path}" > "${z_stat_stdout}" 2> "${z_stat_stderr}"; then
    : # macOS / BSD stat succeeded
  elif stat -c '%a' "${z_path}" > "${z_stat_stdout}" 2> "${z_stat_stderr}"; then
    : # GNU/Linux stat succeeded
  else
    buc_die "${z_varname}: cannot stat ${z_path} — see ${z_stat_stderr}"
  fi

  local z_mode
  z_mode=$(<"${z_stat_stdout}")
  z_mode="${z_mode//$'\n'/}"
  test "${z_mode}" = "600" \
    || buc_die "${z_varname}: mode must be 0600, got ${z_mode}: ${z_path}"

  ssh-keygen -y -P '' -f "${z_path}" \
      > "${z_dry_stdout}" \
      2> "${z_dry_stderr}" \
    || buc_die "${z_varname}: ssh-keygen -y dry-load failed (passphrase-protected or malformed): ${z_path} — see ${z_dry_stderr}"
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Garrison steps (6-step ceremony per BUSJG{B,C,W})

# Step 1 -- open admin SSH (test reachability under key-only auth).
zbujb_garrison_step1_admin_open() {
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  buc_step "  [1/6] Open admin SSH (${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST})"

  local z_exit=0
  zbujb_admin_exec "${z_letter}" <<<'exit 0' || z_exit=$?
  if test "${z_exit}" -ne 0; then
    case "${BURN_PLATFORM}" in
      bubep_windows)
        buc_die "Admin SSH failed (exit ${z_exit}). Run fenestrate first: tt/buw-jpF.Fenestrate.sh ${BURP_VICEROYALTY}"
        ;;
      *)
        buc_die "Admin SSH failed (exit ${z_exit}). Place admin pubkey via 'ssh-copy-id -i ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE}.pub ${BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER}@${BURN_HOST}' first."
        ;;
    esac
  fi
}

# Step 2 -- destroy any existing workload account + home.
zbujb_garrison_step2_destroy() {
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  local z_wlu="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
  local z_wlhome
  z_wlhome=$(zbujb_workload_home "${z_letter}")
  buc_step "  [2/6] Destroy workload (${z_wlu})"

  case "${z_letter}" in
    b)
      zbujb_admin_exec b <<SCRIPT
set -uo pipefail
sudo -n userdel -r '${z_wlu}' 2>/dev/null || true
sudo -n rm -rf '${z_wlhome}' 2>/dev/null || true
SCRIPT
      ;;
    c)
      # Cygwin workload is a Windows local user; delete via net.exe then
      # purge the Cygwin home directory.
      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
set -uo pipefail
net.exe user '${z_wlu}' /delete > /dev/null 2>&1 || true
rm -rf '${z_wlhome}'                              2>/dev/null || true
rm -rf '/cygdrive/c/cygwin64/home/${z_wlu}'       2>/dev/null || true
SCRIPT
      ;;
    w)
      zbujb_admin_exec w <<SCRIPT
set -uo pipefail
userdel -r '${z_wlu}' 2>/dev/null || true
rm -rf '${z_wlhome}'  2>/dev/null || true
SCRIPT
      ;;
  esac
}

# Step 3 -- create the workload account fresh, ssh-only, no privilege.
zbujb_garrison_step3_create() {
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  local z_wlu="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
  buc_step "  [3/6] Create workload (${z_wlu})"

  case "${z_letter}" in
    b)
      case "${BURN_PLATFORM}" in
        bubep_linux)
          zbujb_admin_exec b <<SCRIPT
set -euo pipefail
sudo -n useradd --create-home --shell /bin/bash '${z_wlu}'
sudo -n passwd  --lock '${z_wlu}'
SCRIPT
          ;;
        bubep_mac)
          # Mac uses dscl/sysadminctl; left for in-environment refinement.
          # Operator may need to seat a more idiomatic primary group ID.
          zbujb_admin_exec b <<SCRIPT
set -euo pipefail
sudo -n sysadminctl -addUser '${z_wlu}' -roleAccount
sudo -n dscl . -create '/Users/${z_wlu}' UserShell /bin/bash
SCRIPT
          ;;
      esac
      ;;
    c)
      # Cygwin reflects Windows user accounts; mint via net.exe with a
      # disabled-password posture (we want ssh-key-only).
      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
set -euo pipefail
net.exe user '${z_wlu}' /add /passwordreq:no /active:yes > /dev/null
mkpasswd -l -u '${z_wlu}' >> /etc/passwd
mkdir -p '/home/${z_wlu}'
chown -R '${z_wlu}' '/home/${z_wlu}'
SCRIPT
      ;;
    w)
      zbujb_admin_exec w <<SCRIPT
set -euo pipefail
useradd --create-home --shell /bin/bash '${z_wlu}'
passwd  --lock '${z_wlu}'
SCRIPT
      ;;
  esac
}

# Step 4 -- write workload authorized_keys with the shell-letter command=
# directive and the workload pubkey (derived locally from the privkey).
zbujb_garrison_step4_place_trust() {
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  local z_wlu="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
  local z_wlhome
  z_wlhome=$(zbujb_workload_home "${z_letter}")
  buc_step "  [4/6] Place workload trust (${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys)"

  local z_command_directive
  z_command_directive=$(bujb_command_for "${z_letter}")

  local z_pubkey
  z_pubkey=$(ssh-keygen -y -P '' -f "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "ssh-keygen -y failed for workload key: ${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"

  local z_authkeys_line="${z_command_directive} ${z_pubkey}"

  case "${z_letter}" in
    b|w)
      zbujb_admin_exec "${z_letter}" <<SCRIPT
set -euo pipefail
$([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")mkdir -p   '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
$([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")chmod 700  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
echo '${z_authkeys_line}' | $([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")tee '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys' > /dev/null
$([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")chmod 600  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys'
$([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ")chown -R '${z_wlu}:${z_wlu}' '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
SCRIPT
      ;;
    c)
      # In Cygwin, file ownership/permissions interplay with NTFS ACLs.
      # mkdir/chmod/chown are Cygwin-mediated; run as the admin user
      # without sudo (Windows admins already have privilege).
      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
set -euo pipefail
mkdir -p   '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
chmod 700  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
echo '${z_authkeys_line}' > '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys'
chmod 600  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys'
chown -R '${z_wlu}' '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
SCRIPT
      ;;
  esac
}

# Step 5 -- copy workload privkey to the remote at the shell-letter's
# hardcoded destination path, with workload ownership and 0600 mode.
zbujb_garrison_step5_plant_key() {
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  local z_wlu="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
  local z_wlhome
  z_wlhome=$(zbujb_workload_home "${z_letter}")
  local z_keypath
  z_keypath=$(bujb_workload_keypath_for "${z_letter}")
  local z_target="${z_wlhome}/${z_keypath}"

# Step 5 -- copy workload privkey to the remote at the shell-letter's
# hardcoded destination path, with workload ownership and 0600 mode.
zbujb_garrison_step5_plant_key() {
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  local z_wlu="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
  local z_wlhome
  z_wlhome=$(zbujb_workload_home "${z_letter}")
  local z_keypath
  z_keypath=$(bujb_workload_keypath_for "${z_letter}")
  local z_target="${z_wlhome}/${z_keypath}"
  buc_step "  [5/6] Plant workload privkey (${z_target})"

  local z_key_b64
  z_key_b64=$(base64 < "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "base64 encode failed for workload key: ${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"

  case "${z_letter}" in
    b|w)
      local z_sudo=""
      test "${z_letter}" = "b" && z_sudo="sudo -n"
      zbujb_admin_exec "${z_letter}" <<SCRIPT
set -euo pipefail
ztmp=\$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "\${ztmp}"' EXIT
echo '${z_key_b64}' | base64 -d > "\${ztmp}"
${z_sudo} mkdir -p   '$(dirname "${z_target}")'
${z_sudo} install -m 600 -o '${z_wlu}' -g '${z_wlu}' "\${ztmp}" '${z_target}'
SCRIPT
      ;;
    c)
      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
set -euo pipefail
ztmp=\$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "\${ztmp}"' EXIT
echo '${z_key_b64}' | base64 -d > "\${ztmp}"
mkdir -p   '$(dirname "${z_target}")'
cp "\${ztmp}" '${z_target}'
chmod 600  '${z_target}'
chown      '${z_wlu}' '${z_target}'
SCRIPT
      ;;
  esac
}

# Step 6 -- workload-side round-trip validation (knock).
zbujb_garrison_step6_validate() {
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  buc_step "  [6/6] Validate workload round-trip"

  local z_exit=0
  ssh -i "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}"     \
      -o IdentitiesOnly=yes                       \
      -o BatchMode=yes                            \
      -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new         \
      -o ConnectTimeout=10                        \
      "${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}@${BURN_HOST}" \
      true                                        \
    || z_exit=$?

  test "${z_exit}" -eq 0 \
    || buc_die "Workload round-trip failed (ssh exit ${z_exit}); the new account did not accept its own key."
}

######################################################################
# Public: Garrison ceremony

# bujb_garrison LETTER -- run the 6-step garrison ceremony for shell-letter
# b, c, or w. Caller must have invoked bujb_resolve_investiture beforehand.
bujb_garrison() {
  zbujb_sentinel
  test "${ZBUJB_RESOLVED:-}" = "1" \
    || buc_die "bujb_garrison: call bujb_resolve_investiture first"

  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  zbujb_garrison_assert_platform "${z_letter}"

  buc_step "Garrison-${z_letter}: ${BURP_VICEROYALTY} (${BURN_HOST})"

      ;;

      # without sudo (Windows admins already have privilege).
      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
set -euo pipefail
mkdir -p   '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
chmod 700  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
echo '${z_authkeys_line}' > '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys'
chmod 600  '${z_wlhome}/.ssh/authorized_keys'
chown -R '${z_wlu}' '${z_wlhome}/.ssh'
SCRIPT
      ;;
  esac
}

zbujb_garrison_step5_plant_key() {
  local z_letter="${1:-}"
  local z_wlu="${BURC_WORKLOAD_USER}"
  local z_wlhome
  z_wlhome=$(zbujb_workload_home_capture "${z_letter}") \
    || buc_die "step5: workload home unresolvable for letter='${z_letter}' platform='${BURN_PLATFORM}'"
  local z_keypath
  z_keypath=$(bujb_workload_keypath_for_capture "${z_letter}") \
    || buc_die "step5: bujb_workload_keypath_for_capture failed for letter='${z_letter}'"
  local z_target="${z_wlhome}/${z_keypath}"
  local z_target_dir="${z_target%/*}"  # parameter expansion replaces dirname
  buc_step "  [5/6] Plant workload privkey (${z_target})"

  base64 < "${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE}" \
      > "${ZBUJB_KEY_B64_STDOUT}" \
      2> "${ZBUJB_KEY_B64_STDERR}" \
    || buc_die "base64 encode failed for workload key: ${BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE} — see ${ZBUJB_KEY_B64_STDERR}"
  local z_key_b64
  z_key_b64=$(<"${ZBUJB_KEY_B64_STDOUT}")
  z_key_b64="${z_key_b64//$'\n'/}"

  local z_sudo_prefix=""
  test "${z_letter}" = "b" && z_sudo_prefix="sudo -n "

  case "${z_letter}" in
    b|w)
      zbujb_admin_exec "${z_letter}" <<SCRIPT
set -euo pipefail
ztmp=\$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "\${ztmp}"' EXIT
echo '${z_key_b64}' | base64 -d > "\${ztmp}"
${z_sudo_prefix}mkdir -p   '${z_target_dir}'
${z_sudo_prefix}install -m 600 -o '${z_wlu}' -g '${z_wlu}' "\${ztmp}" '${z_target}'
SCRIPT
      ;;
    c)
      zbujb_admin_exec c <<SCRIPT
set -euo pipefail
ztmp=\$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "\${ztmp}"' EXIT
echo '${z_key_b64}' | base64 -d > "\${ztmp}"
mkdir -p   '${z_target_dir}'
cp "\${ztmp}" '${z_target}'
chmod 600  '${z_target}'
chown      '${z_wlu}' '${z_target}'
SCRIPT
      ;;
  esac
}

params.intent: L3f BCG capture/stderr cleanup in bujb_jurisdiction.sh + L4 spec command= literal lock-in to BUSJG{B,C,W}. Eighteen BCG line-502/529 violations cleared in bujb_jurisdiction.sh: (1) zbujb_check_key_file rewritten with priv|work slot parameter — stat output now captures via per-slot temp files (ZBUJB_STAT_STDOUT/STDERR_{PRIV,WORK}) replacing the $(stat ... 2>/dev/null) pattern, ssh-keygen -y dry-load now captures via ZBUJB_DRYLOAD_STDOUT/STDERR_{PRIV,WORK} replacing >/dev/null 2>&1, both forensic-preserving with die messages referencing the stderr file path; (2) garrison step4 ssh-keygen -y for workload pubkey now captures via ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDOUT/STDERR_WORK temp files, $(<file) bash builtin reads the pubkey, parameter expansion strips trailing newline; (3) garrison step5 base64 capture now uses ZBUJB_KEY_B64_STDOUT/STDERR temp files; (4) fenestrate phase1 ssh-keygen -y for admin pubkey now captures via ZBUJB_PUBKEY_STDOUT/STDERR_PRIV; (5) garrison step5 dirname externals at two heredoc sites replaced with parameter expansion local z_target_dir=${z_target%/*}; (6) garrison step4/step5 sudo-prefix dance refactored from inline $([ "${z_letter}" = "b" ] && echo "sudo -n ") to a precomputed local z_sudo_prefix var (eliminating five inline $() captures in step4 and matching step5's restructure); (7) three internal helpers renamed to _capture form per BCG line 502 exemption — bujb_command_for→bujb_command_for_capture, bujb_workload_keypath_for→bujb_workload_keypath_for_capture, zbujb_workload_home→zbujb_workload_home_capture — with case-default buc_die replaced by return 1 and all callers (5 sites in steps 2/4/5) updated to two-line $() capture with explicit || buc_die. Twelve new readonly Z* temp file constants added to zbujb_kindle for the stat/dryload/pubkey/base64 captures, paired stdout+stderr per capture site for forensic preservation. Zero callers outside bujb_jurisdiction.sh — verified via grep across Tools/ and tt/ — so the renames are scope-contained. BUS0/BUSJG{B,C,W} command= literal lock-in: BUSJGB/BUSJGC/BUSJGW step 4 now inline the literal command= directive string for shell-letter b/c/w as a [listing] block immediately after the step prose, with a one-line note that the value is locked spec content mirrored at BUJB_command_{b,c,w} in bujb_jurisdiction. Verified byte-for-byte equality between the three spec literals and the three BUJB_command_* tinder constants. The module-side comment above BUJB_command_* (already says 'Locked spec content; mirrored in BUSJG{B,C,W}') now matches truth. Verification: bash -n green; tt/buw-st green (5 fixtures, 28 cases unchanged); tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 (matches BBAAz baseline, lone failure rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels unchanged); buw-jpF/jpGb/jwk smoke-tests dispatch and die cleanly on missing investiture. Per-verb platform assertion location (per-verb helpers inside public verb function rather than inline at tabtarget) left as-is per operator decision. Internal-function $()-on-non-_capture violations were the bulk of this sweep — the BCG line 502 rule grants exemption only to _capture/_recite/bash-introspection, not to ssh/base64/ssh-keygen as the prior docket axis had stated; this notch tightens to the actual BCG wording.

content.text:
## Review of BBAA_ (`base64-to-openssl-sweep`)

### Concerns/suggestions for the BBAA_ docket

**1. Note the BCG temp-file wrapper at the bujb encode site.**
After AAm, `bujb_jurisdiction.sh:480-483` wraps the encode in `> "${ZBUJB_KEY_B64_STDOUT}" 2> "${ZBUJB_KEY_B64_STDERR}" || buc_die`. Mechanical sweep swaps the tool name only — preserve the wrapper. Worth one line so a future agent doesn't strip it back to a one-liner.

**2. `-A` interacts with newline-stripping.**
My encode capture has `z_key_b64="${z_key_b64//$'\n'/}"` (defensive against raw `base64`'s 76-col wrapping). `openssl enc -base64 -A` emits a single line, so the strip becomes a no-op. Docket could note: sweep may drop the post-capture newline-strip for symmetry with basis commit `385589f4`, or leave as cheap defense — either fine, but worth a one-liner so the choice is conscious.

**3. Out-of-scope `Cygwin openssl preflight` is defensible but has a matching spec-side gap.**
Cygwin's default install doesn't bundle openssl. The garrison-c step5 decode heredoc runs on the remote Cygwin shell, so openssl-on-Cygwin is a real precondition. Defer is fine for BBAA_, but the spec side (BUSJGC precondition, or the Windows handbook's Cygwin install step at `tt/buw-HWec`) should pick it up. Suggest: add an itch (`jji_itch.md`) entry to track "Cygwin openssl precondition surfacing" so it doesn't get lost.

**4. Site enumeration confirmed durable.**
Discovery recipe (`grep -rn '\bbase64\b'`) is correct. Current state in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`: one encode site (line 480), two decode sites in heredocs (lines 497, 507). All three were minted by AAm's L3c (per the docket's own "garrison ceremony reintroduced raw calls" line). Operator awareness already documented.

### Work left before wrapping ₢A-AAm

**None.** All three of your decisions (Q1: all 11 → revised to 18 violations folded; Q2: option a inline; Q3: into AAm, no new pace) are landed. Verification green. Notch in. The base64 sites are not AAm's problem — BBAA_ is correctly scoped to its own pace under heat ₣BB and the sweep will work cleanly against AAm's wrapper.

Ready when you are.

2. Add to **Constraints**: with `-A`, encoded output has no embedded newlines; sweep may drop any post-capture newline-strip (`${var//$'\n'/}`).

## Character

Mechanical sweep — convert any raw `base64` invocation in BUK/RBK shell to
the openssl-standardized form established by commit 385589f4. The
jurisdiction garrison ceremony reintroduced raw calls; potentially others
have leaked since.

## Scope

- Rescan `Tools/buk` and `Tools/rbk` for raw `base64` invocations — do
  not trust prior line numbers, files and call sites may have moved.
  Recipe: `grep -rn '\bbase64\b' Tools/{buk,rbk} --include='*.sh'`,
  filter out `openssl ... base64`, jq's `@base64`, and comment-only
  occurrences.
- Replace encode form: `base64 < FILE` → `openssl enc -base64 -A < FILE`
- Replace decode form: `base64 -d` → `openssl enc -base64 -d -A`
- Decode sites inside SSH heredocs run on the remote shell — same
  substitution applies; remote toolchain is presumed sufficient
  (cygwin/wsl/linux/mac all have openssl available).

## Constraints

- Acceptance for the jurisdiction sites is the existing live garrison
  ceremony — `buw-jpG{b,c,w}` step 6 `ssh ... true` already round-trips
  the planted privkey byte-exact; corrupted decode fails SSH auth.
- No new test fixture needed.
- `-A` flag suppresses line-wrapping. Use it uniformly on encode and
  decode so single-line embedding in heredocs stays valid.
- bujb_jurisdiction.sh encode site has a BCG temp-file capture wrapper
  (`> ZBUJB_KEY_B64_STDOUT 2> ZBUJB_KEY_B64_STDERR || buc_die`) —
  preserve it; sweep swaps tool name only.
- With `-A`, encoded output has no embedded newlines; sweep may drop
  any post-capture newline-strip (`${var//$'\n'/}`) that was defensive
  against raw `base64`'s 76-col wrapping.

## Out of scope

- `Tools/lmci/bundle.sh` — different kit, predates the sweep
- jq `@base64` filter — internal to jq, not a bash dependency

params.summary: Implementation of the four-field BURP / BURC_WORKLOAD_USER / bujb_jurisdiction design across L1-L5 plus L3f BCG capture/stderr cleanup and L4 spec command= literal lock-in. L1 reshaped BURP to four fields (VICEROYALTY/PRIVILEGED_USER/PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE/WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE), added BURC_WORKLOAD_USER as project-wide xname, and minted the bub/bube/bubep_* platform tree with BUBC_platforms_{linux,mac,windows} tinder constants; BURN_PLATFORM enum re-enrolled with bubep_* values; BURN_WORKLOADS dropped. L2 introduced bujb_jurisdiction.sh as the BCG-compliant operational seat housing locked spec data (BUJB_command_{b,c,w}, BUJB_workload_keypath_{b,c,w}, BUJB_wsl_distribution=rbtww-main, BUJB_sshd_hardening) and bujb_cli.sh as the furnish/dispatch wrapper, with bujb_resolve_investiture as the sole single-call-per-process load-and-cross-validate entrypoint (mode 0600 + ssh-keygen -y dry-load on both key files). L3 minted 14 tabtargets (buw-rn[lrv], buw-rp[lrv], buw-jpF, buw-jpG[bcw], buw-jw[kcs], buw-hj0) with correct zipper enrollments and channels; ceremonies implement BUSJPF two-phase fenestrate (Windows OpenSSH only, with PowerShell-as-cmd.exe-shell heredoc transport and Restart-Service-disconnect treated as expected phase boundary) and BUSJG{B,C,W} 6-step garrison (admin SSH probe with platform-aware failure pointer, destroy/create workload account per platform, place authorized_keys with shell-letter command= directive, plant workload privkey via base64+heredoc, round-trip validate). L3d cleanup dropped the redundant BUJB_RESOLVED_* publish layer (~55 sites updated to read BURP/BURN/BURC vars directly). L3e relocated 8 pure-literal tinder constants out of zbujb_kindle to module top-level per BCG tinder discipline. L4 swept BUS0 drift (burp_node→burp_viceroyalty, burn_platform Values→bubep_*, sub-letter legend documenting bujb_'s b=bash/p=powershell monosemy), retired the two superseded colophons (buw-HWab AccessBase, buw-HWax AccessEntrypoints) plus their function bodies and zipper enrollments, and rewired the RBK Windows orchestrator (rbhw0_top.sh) to a 6-phase / 11-step setup sequence reflecting the prerequisite chain (SSH reachability → admin trust+harden → environments → workload provisioning → user provisioning → docker). L3f BCG sweep cleared 18 capture/stderr violations in bujb_jurisdiction.sh per BCG line 502/529: zbujb_check_key_file gained a priv|work slot parameter routing stat + ssh-keygen -y dry-load through 8 new ZBUJB_STAT/DRYLOAD_*_{PRIV,WORK} temp-file constants; garrison step4 ssh-keygen pubkey emit + step5 base64 encode + fenestrate phase1 ssh-keygen pubkey emit all moved from $(external) capture to temp-file-then-$(<file) pattern; garrison step5 dirname externals replaced with parameter expansion (z_target_dir=${z_target%/*}); garrison step4/step5 inline $([ z_letter = b ] && echo sudo -n) sudo-prefix dance refactored to precomputed local z_sudo_prefix (eliminating 5 inline $() captures); three internal helpers renamed to _capture form per BCG line 502 exemption (bujb_command_for→bujb_command_for_capture, bujb_workload_keypath_for→bujb_workload_keypath_for_capture, zbujb_workload_home→zbujb_workload_home_capture) with case-default buc_die replaced by return 1 and all 5 caller sites updated to two-line || buc_die discipline. L4 spec command= lock-in inlined the three concrete command= directive strings into BUSJGB/BUSJGC/BUSJGW step 4 as [listing] blocks, byte-for-byte equal to the BUJB_command_{b,c,w} tinder constants, with explicit mirroring notes — making spec the source of truth for the contract content and module the mirror. Verification across the full AAm scope: bash -n green on all edited shell files; tt/buw-st green (5 fixtures, 28 cases unchanged from baseline); tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 25/26 (matches BBAAz baseline, lone failure rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels unchanged); buw-jpF/jpGb/jwk smoke-tests dispatch correctly and die cleanly on missing investiture. Out-of-scope held: PowerShell garrison + dispatch, adopt verb, user regime, Cygwin/WSL sshd hardening verb, broad cleanup sweep (deferred to AAn), and end-to-end Windows-node test (lives in AAD/E/F). Per-verb platform assertion location (per-verb assert helpers inside public verb function rather than inline at tabtarget) accepted as-is by operator decision; the docket's literal 'inline 2-line check' wording is a minor narrative drift to address at next paddock review. Pre-existing rot in tt/buw-hw (buhw_top references undefined BUWZ_RHC_LINUX/MAC/LOCALHOST + BUWZ_HW_VERIFY_SSH + BUWZ_RN_INSTALL_KEY) confirmed dormant, unchanged by AAm's colophon retirement, deferred to AAn broad sweep. The 3 raw base64 sites preserved in bujb step5 (1 encode + 2 decode in heredocs) are out-of-scope for AAm and will be swept by ₢BBAA_ base64-to-openssl-sweep — that pace's docket was updated to note AAm's BCG temp-file wrapper around the encode site must be preserved through the mechanical tool swap.
